Rule Variations
All competitions in the Formula SAE Series may post rule variations specific to the operation of
the events in their countries. Vehicle design requirements and restrictions will remain
unchanged. Any rule variations will be posted on the websites specific to those competitions.

input: What does rule AD.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Official Announcements and Competition Information
Teams must read the published announcements by SAE International and the other organizing
bodies and be familiar with all official announcements concerning the competitions and any
released rules interpretations.

input: What does rule AD.1.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Official Languages
The official language of the Formula SAE series is English.
Document submissions, presentations and discussions in English are acceptable at all
competitions in the series.

Team members must be enrolled as degree seeking undergraduate or graduate students in

Team members who have graduated during the seven month period prior to the competition

input: What does rule AD.3.1.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Teams which are formed with members from two or more universities are treated as a single
team. A student at any university making up the team may compete at any competition
where the team participates. The multiple universities are treated as one university with the
same eligibility requirements.

input: What does rule AD.3.1.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Each team member may participate at a competition for only one team. This includes
competitions where the University enters both IC and EV teams.

input: What does rule AD.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Age
Team members must be minimum 18 years of age.

input: What does rule AD.3.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Driver’s License
Team members who will drive a competition vehicle at any time during a competition must
hold a valid, government issued driver’s license.

input: What does rule AD.3.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Society Membership
Team members must be members of SAE International
Proof of membership, such as membership card, is required at the competition.

input: What does rule AD.3.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Medical Insurance
Individual medical insurance coverage is required and is the sole responsibility of the
participant.

input: What does rule AD.3.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:

Team members who require accessibility for areas outside of ADA Compliance must contact

input: What does rule AD.5.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Electrical System Advisor (ESA) must be a professionally competent person(s) nominated
by the team who can advise on the electrical and control systems that will be integrated into
the vehicle. The faculty advisor may also be the ESA if all the requirements below are met.

input: What does rule AD.5.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The ESA must supply details of their experience of electrical and/or control systems
engineering as used in the vehicle on the ESO/ESA form for approval by the organizers.

input: What does rule AD.5.3.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The ESA must be sufficiently qualified to advise the team on their proposed electrical and
control system designs based on significant experience of the technology being developed and
its implementation into vehicles or other safety critical systems. More than one person may
be needed.

input: What does rule AD.5.3.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The ESA must advise the team on the merits of any relevant engineering solutions. Solutions
should be discussed, questioned and approved before they are implemented into the final
vehicle design.

input: What does rule AD.5.3.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The ESA should advise the students on any required training to work with the systems on the
vehicle.

input: What does rule AD.5.3.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The ESA must review the Electrical System Form and to confirm that in principle the vehicle
has been designed using good engineering practices.

input: What does rule AD.5.3.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The ESA must ensure that the team communicates any unusual aspects of the design to the
organizers to reduce the risk of exclusion or significant changes being required to pass
Technical Inspection.

input: What does rule AD.6.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Registration fees must be paid to the organizer by the deadline specified on the respective
competition website.

input: What does rule AD.6.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule

output:
Waitlisted teams must submit all documents by the same deadlines as registered teams to

input: What does rule AD.6.4.2 is about? return only the text of the rule

Once a team withdraws from the competition, the organizer will inform the next team on the

input: What does rule AD.6.4.3 is about? return only the text of the rule

to have the registration payment completed or in process.

input: What does rule AD.6.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:

Registered teams that will not attend the competition must inform the organizer, as posted on
the Event Website.

Personal cars and trailers must be parked in designated areas only. Only authorized vehicles
will be allowed in the track areas.

output:

The use of motorcycles, quads, bicycles, scooters, skateboards, rollerblades or similar person-
carrying devices by team members and spectators in any part of the competition area,
including the paddocks, is prohibited.

input: What does rule AD.7.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Self-propelled Pit Carts, Tool Boxes, etc. - Prohibited
The use of self-propelled pit carts, tool boxes, tire carriers or similar motorized devices in any
part of the competition site, including the paddocks, is prohibited.

Dynamic Events and Maximum Scores
Acceleration 100 points
Skid Pad 75 points
Autocross 125 points
Efficiency 100 points
Endurance 275 points
Total 675 points

input: What does rule D.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Definitions

input: What does rule D.1.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Dynamic Area – Any designated portion(s) of the competition site where the vehicles may
move under their own power. This includes competition, inspection and practice areas.

input: What does rule D.1.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Staging Area – Any area(s) inside the Dynamic Area prior to the entry to an event for the
purpose of gathering those vehicles that are about to start.

input: What does rule D.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule D.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Vehicle Movement

input: What does rule D.2.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Outside of the Dynamic Area(s), vehicles must be pushed at a normal walking pace using the
Push Bar (VE.2.2), with a driver in the cockpit and with another team member walking beside

input: What does rule D.2.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The team may move the vehicle with
a. All four wheels on the ground
b. The rear wheels supported on dollies, by push bar mounted wheels
The external wheels supporting the rear of the vehicle must be non pivoting so the
vehicle travels only where the front wheels are steered. The driver must always be able
to steer and brake the vehicle normally.

input: What does rule D.2.1.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: When the Push Bar is attached, the engine must stay off, unless authorized by the officials

input: What does rule D.2.1.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Vehicles must be Shutdown when being moved around the paddock

input: What does rule D.2.1.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Vehicles with wings must have two team members, one walking on each side of the vehicle
when the vehicle is being pushed

input: What does rule D.2.1.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: A 25 point penalty may be assessed for each violation

input: What does rule D.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Fueling and Charging
(IC only) Officials must conduct all fueling activities in the designated location.
(EV only) Accumulator charging must be done in the designated location EV.11.5

input: What does rule D.2.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Powertrain Operation
In the paddock, (IC) Engines may be run or (EV) Tractive System may be Active if all three:
a. The vehicle has passed Technical Inspection up to and including the Tilt Test OR a
Technical Inspector gives permission
b. The vehicle is supported on a stand
c. The drive wheels are minimum 10 cm off the ground, OR the drive wheels are removed

output:
Drivers Meetings – Attendance Required
All drivers for an event must attend the drivers meeting(s). The driver for an event will be
disqualified if he/she does not attend the driver meeting for the event.

output:

Refer to the Event Website for specific information

output:
The organizer may specify restrictions for the Dynamic Area. These could include limiting the
number of team members and what may be brought into the area.

input: What does rule D.3.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The organizer may specify additional restrictions for the Staging Area. These could include

input: What does rule D.3.2.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The organizer may establish requirements for persons in the Dynamic Area, such as closed toe

Vehicles must move under their own power only when inside the designated Dynamic Area(s),

input: What does rule D.3.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule

Driving a vehicle outside of scheduled events or scheduled practice will result in a 200 point

input: What does rule D.3.10 is about? return only the text of the rule

output:
If a vehicle stalls and cannot restart without external assistance, or is damaged and not able to
complete the run, it will be scored DNF for that run

input: What does rule D.3.10.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Disabled vehicles will be cleared from the track by the track workers.

input: What does rule D.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule D.4.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Command Flags

input: What does rule D.4.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Any Command Flag must be obeyed immediately and without question.

input: What does rule D.4.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Black Flag - Pull into the Driver Change Area for discussion with the track officials. A time
penalty may be assessed.

input: What does rule D.4.1.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Black Flag with Orange Dot - Pull into the Driver Change Area for a mechanical inspection,
something has been observed that needs closer inspection.

input: What does rule D.4.1.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Blue Flag - Pull into the designated passing zone to be passed by a faster competitor. Obey the
corner workers signals at the end of the passing zone to merge into competition.

input: What does rule D.4.1.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Checkered Flag - Run has been completed. Exit the course at the designated point.

input: What does rule D.4.1.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Green Flag – Approval to begin your run, enter the course under direction of the starter. If
you stall the vehicle, please restart and await another Green Flag

input: What does rule D.4.1.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Red Flag - Come to an immediate safe controlled stop on the course. Pull to the side of the
course as much as possible to keep the course open. Follow corner worker directions.

input: What does rule D.4.1.8 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Yellow Flag (Stationary) - Danger, SLOW DOWN, be prepared to take evasive action,
something has happened beyond the flag station. NO PASSING unless directed by the corner
workers.

input: What does rule D.4.1.9 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Yellow Flag (Waved) - Great Danger, SLOW DOWN, evasive action is most likely required, BE
PREPARED TO STOP, something has happened beyond the flag station, NO PASSING unless
directed by the corner workers.

input: What does rule D.4.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Informational Flags

input: What does rule D.4.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: An Information Flag communicates to the driver, but requires no specific action.

input: What does rule D.4.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Red and Yellow Striped Flag - Something is on the racing surface that should not be there. Be
prepared for evasive maneuvers to avoid the situation.

input: What does rule D.4.2.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
White Flag - There is a slow moving vehicle on the course. Be prepared to approach it at a
cautious rate.

Skidpad Event ................................................................................................................................... 130
SKIDPAD EVENT
The Skidpad event measures the vehicle cornering ability on a flat surface while making a
constant radius turn.

input: What does rule D.10.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Skidpad Layout

input: What does rule D.10.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Course Design
• Two pairs of concentric circles in a figure of eight pattern
• Centers of the circles 18.25 m apart
• Inner circles 15.25 m in diameter
• Outer circles 21.25 m in diameter
• Driving path the 3.0 m wide path between the inner and outer circles

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 131 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule D.10.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Cone Placement
a. Sixteen (16) pylons will be placed around the inside of each inner circle and thirteen (13)
pylons will be positioned around the outside of each outer circle in the pattern shown in
the Skidpad layout diagram.
b. Each circle will be marked with a chalk line, inside the inner circle and outside the outer
circle
The Skidpad layout diagram shows the circles for cone placement, not for course marking.
Chalk lines are marked on the opposite side of the cones, outside the driving path
c. Additional pylons will establish the entry and exit gates.
d. A cone may be placed in the middle of the exit gate until the finish lap.

input: What does rule D.10.1.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Course Operation
a. Vehicles will enter and exit through gates on a 3.0 m wide path that is tangential to the
circles where they meet.
b. The line between the centers of the circles defines the start/stop line.
c. A lap is defined as traveling around one of the circles from the start/stop line and
returning to the start/stop line.

input: What does rule D.10.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Skidpad Procedure

input: What does rule D.10.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Each team may attempt up to four runs, using two drivers, limited to two runs for each driver.

input: What does rule D.10.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Runs with the first driver have priority

input: What does rule D.10.2.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Each Skidpad run is performed as follows:
a. A Green Flag or light signal will give the approval to begin the run
b. The vehicle will enter perpendicular to the figure eight and will take one full lap on the
right circle
c. The next lap will be on the right circle and will be timed

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 132 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
d. Immediately following the second lap, the vehicle will enter the left circle for the third
lap
e. The fourth lap will be on the left circle and will be timed
f. Immediately upon finishing the fourth lap, the vehicle will exit the track. The exit is at the
intersection moving in the same direction as entered

input: What does rule D.10.2.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Each driver may go to the front of the staging line immediately after their first run to make a
second run

output: Skidpad Penalties

input: What does rule D.10.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Cones (DOO)
A 0.125 second penalty for each DOO (including entry and exit gate cones) on that run

input: What does rule D.10.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Off Course (OC)
DNF for that run. Vehicles that stall or spin out may continue if they have not gone Off
Course.

input: What does rule D.10.3.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:

Vehicles that run an incorrect number of laps or run the laps in the wrong sequence will be

output: Skidpad Scoring

input: What does rule D.10.4.1 is about? return only the text of the rule

Scoring Term Definitions
• Corrected Time = ( Right Lap Time + Left Lap Time ) / 2 + ( DOO * 0.125 )
• Tyour - the best Corrected Time for the team
• Tmin - is the lowest Corrected Time recorded for any team
• Tmax - 125% of Tmin

input: What does rule D.10.4.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
When Tyour < Tmax. the team score is calculated as:
Skidpad Score = 71.5 x ( Tmax / Tyour )2 -1 + 3.5
( Tmax / Tmin )2 -1

input: What does rule D.10.4.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: When Tyour > Tmax , Skidpad Score = 3.5

The Endurance event will consist of multiple laps over a closed course to a total distance of

The Endurance Run Order is established to let vehicles of similar speed potential be on track
together to reduce the need for passing.

The Endurance Run Order:

The team may bring only the following into the Driver Change Area:

c. Minimal tools necessary to adjust the vehicle to fit the second driver and/or change tires

The Driver Change will be performed in this sequence:

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 136 of 140

b. First Driver turns off the engine / Tractive System. Driver Change time starts.

yielding for passing, not driving inside the designated course, etc.

c. The time spent in Black Flag or a time penalty may be included in the Endurance Run

input: What does rule D.12.13.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Scoring Term Definitions:
• Endurance Run Time - Total Time for both Drivers, minus the Driver Change lap, minus
any Mechanical Black Flag Time, plus any Penalty time D.14.2
• Corrected Time = Endurance Run Time + ( DOO * 2 ) + ( OC * 20 )
• Tyour - the Corrected Time for the team
• Tmin - the lowest Corrected Time recorded for any team
• Tmax - 145% of Tmin

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 138 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule D.12.13.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The vehicle must complete the Endurance Event to receive a score based on their Corrected
Time
a. If Tyour < Tmax, the team score is calculated as:
Endurance Time Score = 250 x ( Tmax / Tyour ) -1
( Tmax / Tmin ) -1
b. If Tyour > Tmax, Endurance Time Score = 0

input: What does rule D.12.13.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:

input: What does rule D.12.13.4 is about? return only the text of the rule

Endurance Score = Endurance Time Score + Endurance Laps Score

input: What does rule EV.4.9.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Every housing or enclosure containing parts of the Tractive System other than Motor
housings, must be labelled with the:
a. Symbol specified in ISO 7010-W012 (triangle with black lightning bolt on yellow
background)
b. Text “High Voltage” if the voltage meets T.9.1.1

input: What does rule EV.4.9.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
If the material of the housing containing parts of the Tractive System is electrically conductive,
it must have a low resistance connection to GLV System Ground, see EV.6.7

input: What does rule EV.4.10 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Accumulator Hand Cart

input: What does rule EV.4.10.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Teams must have a Hand Cart to transport their Accumulator Container(s)

input: What does rule EV.4.10.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Hand Cart must be used when the Accumulator Container(s) are transported on the
competition site EV.11.4.2 EV.11.5.1

input: What does rule EV.4.10.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Hand Cart must:
a. Be able to carry the load of the Accumulator Container(s) without tipping over
b. Contain a minimum of two wheels
c. Have a brake that must be:
• Released only using a dead man type switch (where the brake is always on except
when released by pushing and holding a handle) or by manually lifting part of the
cart off the ground
• Able to stop the Hand Cart with a fully loaded Accumulator Container

input: What does rule EV.4.10.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Accumulator Container(s) must be securely attached to the Hand Cart

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 93 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule EV.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule EV.5.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Accumulator

input: What does rule EV.5.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All cells or super capacitors which store the Tractive System energy are built into Accumulator
Segments and must be enclosed in (an) Accumulator Container(s).

input: What does rule EV.5.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Each Accumulator Segment must contain:
• Maximum static voltage of less than 120 V DC
• Maximum energy of 6 MJ
The contained energy of a stack is calculated by multiplying the maximum stack voltage with
the nominal capacity of the used cell(s).

input: What does rule EV.5.1.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
No further energy storage except for reasonably sized intermediate circuit capacitors are
allowed after the Energy Meter EV.3.1

input: What does rule EV.5.1.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All Accumulator Segments and/or Accumulator Containers (including spares and replacement
parts) must be identical to the design documented in the ESF and SES

input: What does rule EV.5.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Electrical Configuration

input: What does rule EV.5.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: All Tractive System components must be rated for the maximum Tractive System voltage

input: What does rule EV.5.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
If the Accumulator Container is made from an electrically conductive material:
a. The poles of the Accumulator Segment(s) and/or cells must be isolated from the inner
wall of the Accumulator Container with an insulating material that is rated for the
maximum Tractive System voltage.
b. All conductive surfaces on the outside of the Accumulator Container must have a low
resistance connection to the GLV System Ground, see EV.6.7
c. Any conductive penetrations, such as mounting hardware, must be protected against
puncturing the insulating barrier.

input: What does rule EV.5.2.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Each Accumulator Segment must be electrically insulated with suitable Nonflammable
Material (F.1.18) (not air) for the two:
a. Between the segments in the container
b. On top of the segment
The intent is to prevent arc flashes caused by inter segment contact or by parts/tools
accidentally falling into the container during maintenance for example.

input: What does rule EV.5.2.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Soldering electrical connections in the high current path is prohibited
Soldering wires to cells for the voltage monitoring input of the AMS is allowed, these wires are
not part of the high current path.

input: What does rule EV.5.2.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Every wire used in an Accumulator Container, whether it is part of the GLV or Tractive System,
must be rated to the maximum Tractive System voltage.

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 94 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule EV.5.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Maintenance Plugs

input: What does rule EV.5.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Maintenance Plugs must allow electrical separation of the Accumulator Segments to meet:
a. The separated Segments meet voltage and energy limits of EV.5.1.2
b. The separation must affect both poles of the Segment

input: What does rule EV.5.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Maintenance Plugs must:
a. Require the physical removal or separation of a component. Contactors or switches are
not acceptable Maintenance Plugs
b. Have access after opening the Accumulator Container and not necessary to move or
remove any other components
c. Not be physically possible to connect in any configuration other than the design intended
configuration
d. Not require tools to install or remove
e. Include a positive locking feature which prevents the plug from unintentionally becoming
loose
f. Be nonconductive on surfaces that do not provide any electrical connection

input: What does rule EV.5.3.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
When the Accumulator Containers are opened or Segments are removed, the Accumulator
Segments must be separated by using the Maintenance Plugs. See EV.11.4.1

input: What does rule EV.5.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Accumulator Isolation Relays - AIR

input: What does rule EV.5.4.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Every Accumulator Container must contain minimum one fuse (EV.6.6) and two or more
Accumulator Isolation Relays (AIR)

input: What does rule EV.5.4.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Accumulator Isolation Relays must:
a. Be a Normally Open type
b. Open both poles of the Accumulator

input: What does rule EV.5.4.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
When the AIRs are open, High Voltage T.9.1.1 must not be external of the Accumulator
Container

input: What does rule EV.5.4.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Accumulator Isolation Relays and any fuses must be separated from the rest of the
Accumulator with an electrically insulated and Nonflammable Material (F.1.18).

input: What does rule EV.5.4.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
A capacitor may be used to hold the AIRs closed for up to 250 ms after the Shutdown Circuit
Opens EV.7.2.2

input: What does rule EV.5.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
High Voltage Disconnect - HVD
A High Voltage Disconnect (HVD) must be included to quickly disconnect one or both poles of
the Accumulator EV.11.3.2

input: What does rule EV.5.5.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The High Voltage Disconnect (HVD) must be:
a. A directly accessible element, fuse or connector
b. More than 350 mm from the ground
c. Easily visible when standing behind the vehicle
d. Operable in 10 seconds or less by an untrained person
e. Operable without removing any bodywork or obstruction or using tools
f. Directly operated. Remote operation through a long handle, rope or wire is not
acceptable.

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 95 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
g. Clearly marked with "HVD"

input: What does rule EV.5.5.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: An Interlock EV.7.8 must Open the Shutdown Circuit EV.7.2.2 when the HVD is removed

input: What does rule EV.5.5.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: A dummy connector or similar may be used to restore isolation to meet EV.6.1.2

input: What does rule EV.5.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Precharge and Discharge Circuits

input: What does rule EV.5.6.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Accumulator must contain a Precharge Circuit. The Precharge Circuit must:
a. Be able to charge the Intermediate Circuit to minimum 90% of the Accumulator voltage
before closing the second AIR
b. Be supplied from the Shutdown Circuit EV.7.1
c. Not be fused

input: What does rule EV.5.6.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Intermediate Circuit must precharge before closing the second AIR. The end of precharge
must be controlled by one of the following two options:
a. Feedback by monitoring the voltage in the Intermediate Circuit
b. A conservative time defined by the longer of:
• Twice the time to charge to 90%
• The time to charge to 90% plus 500ms

input: What does rule EV.5.6.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Tractive System must contain a Discharge Circuit. The Discharge Circuit must be:
a. Wired in a way that it is always active when the Shutdown Circuit is open
b. Able to discharge the Intermediate Circuit capacitors if the HVD has been opened
c. Not be fused
d. Designed to handle the maximum Tractive System voltage for minimum 15 seconds

input: What does rule EV.5.6.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Positive Temperature Coefficient (PTC) devices must not be used to limit current for the
Precharge Circuit or Discharge Circuit

input: What does rule EV.5.6.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The precharge relay must be a mechanical type relay

input: What does rule EV.5.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Voltage Indicator
Each Accumulator Container must have a prominent indicator when High Voltage T.9.1.1 is
present at the vehicle side of the AIRs

input: What does rule EV.5.7.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Voltage Indicator must always function, including when the Accumulator Container is
disconnected or removed

input: What does rule EV.5.7.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The voltage being present at the connectors must directly control the Voltage Indicator using
hard wired electronics with no software control.

input: What does rule EV.5.7.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The control signal which closes the AIRs must not control the Voltage Indicator

input: What does rule EV.5.7.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Voltage Indicator must:
a. Be located where it is clearly visible when connecting/disconnecting the Accumulator
Tractive System connections
b. Be labeled “High Voltage Present”

input: What does rule EV.5.8 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Tractive System Measuring Points - TSMP

input: What does rule EV.5.8.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Two Tractive System Measuring Points (TSMP) must be installed in the vehicle which are:
a. Connected to the positive and negative motor controller/inverter supply lines
b. Next to the Master Switches EV.7.9

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 96 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
c. Protected by a nonconductive housing that can be opened without tools
d. Protected from being touched with bare hands / fingers once the housing is opened

input: What does rule EV.5.8.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Two TSMPs must be installed in the Charger EV.8.2 which are:
a. Connected to the positive and negative Charger output lines
b. Available during charging of any Accumulator(s)

input: What does rule EV.5.8.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The TSMPs must be:
a. 4 mm shrouded banana jacks rated to an appropriate voltage level
b. Color: Red
c. Marked “HV+” and “HV-“

input: What does rule EV.5.8.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Each TSMP must be secured with a current limiting resistor.
a. The resistor must be sized per the following:
Maximum TS Voltage (Vmax) Resistor Value
Vmax <= 200 V DC 5 kOhm
200 V DC < Vmax <= 400 V DC 10 kOhm
400 V DC < Vmax <= 600 V DC 15 kOhm
b. Resistor continuous power rating must be more than the power dissipated across the
TSMPs if they are shorted together
c. Direct measurement of the value of the resistor must be possible during Electrical
Technical Inspection.

input: What does rule EV.5.8.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Any TSMP must not contain additional Overcurrent Protection.

input: What does rule EV.5.9 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Tractive System Active Light - TSAL

input: What does rule EV.5.9.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The vehicle must include a Tractive Systems Active Light (TSAL) that must:
a. Illuminate when the GLV System is energized to indicate the status of the Tractive
System
b. Be directly controlled by the voltage present in the Tractive System using hard wired
electronics. Software control is not permitted.
c. Not perform any other functions.

input: What does rule EV.5.9.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The TSAL may be composed of multiple lights inside a single housing

input: What does rule EV.5.9.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
When the voltage outside the Accumulator Container(s) exceeds T.9.1.1, the TSAL must:
a. Be Color: Red
b. Flash with a frequency between 2 Hz and 5 Hz

input: What does rule EV.5.9.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
When the voltage outside the Accumulator Container(s) is below T.9.1.1, the TSAL must:
a. Be Color: Green
b. Stay continuously illuminated

input: What does rule EV.5.9.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The TSAL mounting location must:
a. Be near the Main Hoop at the highest point of the vehicle.
b. Be inside the Rollover Protection Envelope F.1.13
c. Be no lower than 150 mm from the highest point of the Main Hoop.
d. Not allow contact with the driver’s helmet in any circumstances.

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 97 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
e. Not be in proximity to other lights.

input: What does rule EV.5.9.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The TSAL must be visible:
a. From every horizontal direction, except small angles which are blocked by the Main Hoop
b. From a point 1.6 m vertically from ground level, inside a 3 m horizontal radius from the
TSAL
c. In direct sunlight

input: What does rule EV.5.10 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Connectors
Tractive System connectors outside of a housing must meet one of the two:
• Contain an Interlock EV.7.8 which must Open the Shutdown Circuit EV.7.2.2
• Be sealed at Tech Inspection IN.4.7.1

input: What does rule EV.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule EV.6.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Covers

input: What does rule EV.6.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Nonconductive material or covers must prevent inadvertent human contact with any Tractive
System voltage.
Covers must be secure and sufficiently rigid.
Removable Bodywork is not suitable to enclose Tractive System connections.

input: What does rule EV.6.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Contact with any Tractive System connections with a 100 mm long, 6 mm diameter insulated
test probe must not be possible when the Tractive System enclosures are in place.

input: What does rule EV.6.1.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Tractive System components and Accumulator Containers must be protected from moisture,
rain or puddles.
A rating of IP65 is recommended

input: What does rule EV.6.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Insulation

input: What does rule EV.6.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Insulation material must:
a. Be appropriate for the expected surrounding temperatures
b. Have a minimum temperature rating of 90°C

input: What does rule EV.6.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Insulating tape or paint may be part of the insulation, but must not be the only insulation.

input: What does rule EV.6.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Wiring

input: What does rule EV.6.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All wires and terminals and other conductors used in the Tractive System must be sized for the
continuous current they will conduct

input: What does rule EV.6.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All Tractive System wiring must:
a. Be marked with wire gauge, temperature rating and insulation voltage rating.
A serial number or a norm printed on the wire is sufficient if this serial number or norm is
clearly bound to the wire characteristics for example by a data sheet.
b. Have temperature rating more than or equal to 90°C

input: What does rule EV.6.3.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Tractive System wiring must be:
a. Done to professional standards with sufficient strain relief
b. Protected from loosening due to vibration
c. Protected against damage by rotating and / or moving parts

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 98 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
d. Located out of the way of possible snagging or damage

input: What does rule EV.6.3.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Any Tractive System wiring that runs outside of electrical enclosures:
a. Must meet one of:
• Enclosed in separate orange nonconductive conduit
• Use an orange shielded cable.
b. Must meet one of:
• Run in a fully enclosed container. Bodywork is not an enclosure.
• The conduit or shielded cable is securely anchored at each end to allow it to
withstand a force of 200 N without straining the cable end crimp
c. Any shielded cable must have the shield grounded.

input: What does rule EV.6.3.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Wiring that is not part of the Tractive System must not use orange wiring or conduit.

input: What does rule EV.6.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Connections

input: What does rule EV.6.4.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All Tractive System connections must:
a. Be designed to use intentional current paths through conductors designed for electrical
current
b. Not rely on steel bolts to be the primary conductor
c. Not include compressible material such as plastic in the stack-up

input: What does rule EV.6.4.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
If external, uninsulated heat sinks are used, they must be properly grounded to the GLV
System Ground, see EV.6.7

input: What does rule EV.6.4.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Bolted electrical connections in the high current path of the Tractive System must include a
positive locking feature to prevent unintentional loosening
Lock washers or thread locking compounds (Loctite®) or adhesives are not acceptable.
Bolts with nylon patches are allowed for blind connections into OEM components.

input: What does rule EV.6.4.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Information about the electrical connections supporting the high current path must be
available at Electrical Technical Inspection

input: What does rule EV.6.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Voltage Separation

input: What does rule EV.6.5.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Separation of Tractive System and GLV System:
a. The entire Tractive System and GLV System must be completely galvanically separated.
b. The border between Tractive and GLV System is the galvanic isolation between both
systems. Therefore, some components, such as the Motor Controller, may be part of
both systems.

input: What does rule EV.6.5.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
There must be no connection between the Chassis of the vehicle (or any other conductive
surface that might be inadvertently touched by a person), and any part of any Tractive System
circuits.

input: What does rule EV.6.5.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Tractive System and GLV circuits must not run through the same conduit or connector, except
as allowed in EV.7.8.4

input: What does rule EV.6.5.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
GLV Systems other than the AIRs EV.5.4, parts of the Precharge and Discharge Circuits EV.5.6,
HV DC/DC converters, the AMS EV.7.3, the IMD EV.7.6, parts of the TSAL EV.5.9.1 the Energy
Meter EV.3.1 and cooling fans must not be inside the Accumulator Container.

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 99 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule EV.6.5.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Where both Tractive System and GLV are included inside an enclosure, they must meet one of
the two:
a. Be separated by insulating barriers (in addition to the insulation on the wire) made of
moisture resistant, UL recognized or equivalent insulating materials rated for 90° C or
higher (such as Nomex based electrical insulation)
b. Maintain the following spacing through air, or over a surface (similar to those defined in
UL1741):
U < 100 V DC 10 mm
100 V DC < U < 200 V DC 20 mm
U > 200 V DC 30 mm

input: What does rule EV.6.5.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Spacing must be clearly defined. Components and cables capable of movement must be
positively restrained to maintain spacing.

input: What does rule EV.6.5.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
If Tractive System and GLV are on the same circuit board:
a. They must be on separate, clearly defined and clearly marked areas of the board
b. Required spacing related to the spacing between traces / board areas are as follows:
Voltage Over Surface Thru Air (cut in board) Under Conformal Coating
0-50 V DC 1.6 mm 1.6 mm 1 mm
50-150 V DC 6.4 mm 3.2 mm 2 mm
150-300 V DC 9.5 mm 6.4 mm 3 mm
300-600 V DC 12.7 mm 9.5 mm 4 mm

input: What does rule EV.6.5.8 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Teams must be prepared to show spacing on team built equipment
For inaccessible circuitry, spare boards or appropriate photographs must be available for
inspection.

input: What does rule EV.6.5.9 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All connections to external devices such as laptops from a Tractive System component must
include galvanic isolation.

input: What does rule EV.6.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Overcurrent Protection

input: What does rule EV.6.6.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All electrical systems (both Low Voltage and High Voltage) must have appropriate Overcurrent
Protection/Fusing.

input: What does rule EV.6.6.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Unless otherwise allowed in the Rules, all Overcurrent Protection devices must:
a. Be rated for the highest voltage in the systems they protect.
Overcurrent Protection devices used for DC must be rated for DC and must carry a DC
rating equal to or more than the system voltage
b. Have a continuous current rating less than or equal to the continuous current rating of
any electrical component that it protects
c. Have an interrupt current rating higher than the theoretical short circuit current of the
system that it protects

input: What does rule EV.6.6.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Each parallel element of multiple parallel battery cells, capacitors, strings of battery cells,
strings of capacitors, or conductors must have individual Overcurrent Protection.

input: What does rule EV.6.6.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Any conductors (wires, busbars, etc) conducting the entire pack current must meet one of:
a. Be appropriately sized for the total current that the individual Overcurrent Protection
devices could transmit

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 100 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
b. Contain additional Overcurrent Protection to protect the conductors

input: What does rule EV.6.6.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Battery packs with Low Voltage or non voltage rated fusible links for cell connections may be
used when all three conditions are met:
• An Overcurrent Protection device rated at less than or equal to one third the sum of the
parallel fusible links and complying with EV.6.6.2.b above is connected in series.
• The AMS can detect an open fusible link and will Open the Shutdown Circuit EV.7.2.2 if a
fault is detected.
• Fusible link current rating is specified in manufacturer’s data or suitable test data is
provided.

input: What does rule EV.6.6.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
, EV.7.3.5.b

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 5 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
GR - GENERAL REGULATIONS
If conductor ampacity is reduced below the ampacity of the upstream Overcurrent Protection,
the reduced conductor longer than 150 mm must have additional Overcurrent Protection.
This additional Overcurrent Protection must be:
a. 150 mm or less from the source end of the reduced conductor
b. On both positive and negative conductors in the Tractive System
c. On the positive conductor in the Grounded Low Voltage System

input: What does rule EV.6.6.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Cells with internal Overcurrent Protection may be used without external Overcurrent
Protection if suitably rated.
Most cell internal Overcurrent Protection devices are Low Voltage or non voltage rated and
conditions of EV.6.6.5 above will apply.

input: What does rule EV.6.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Grounding

input: What does rule EV.6.7.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Grounding is required for:
a. Parts of the vehicle which are 100 mm or less from any Tractive System component
b. The Tractive System Firewall T.1.9

input: What does rule EV.6.7.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Grounded parts of the vehicle must have a resistance to GLV System Ground less than the
values specified below.
a. Electrically conductive parts 300 mOhms (measured with a current of 1 A)
Examples: parts made of steel, (anodized) aluminum, any other metal parts
b. Parts which may become electrically conductive 5 Ohm
Example: carbon fiber parts
Carbon fiber parts may need special measures such as using copper mesh or similar to
keep the ground resistance below 5 Ohms.

input: What does rule EV.6.7.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Electrical conductivity of any part may be tested by checking any point which is likely to be
conductive.
Where no convenient conductive point is available, an area of coating may be removed.

input: What does rule EV.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule EV.7.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Shutdown Circuit

input: What does rule EV.7.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Shutdown Circuit consists of the following components, connected in series:
a. Accumulator Management System (AMS) EV.7.3
b. Insulation Monitoring Device (IMD) EV.7.6

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 101 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
c. Brake System Plausibility Device (BSPD) EV.7.7
d. Interlocks (as required) EV.7.8
e. Master Switches (GLVMS, TSMS) EV.7.9
f. Shutdown Buttons EV.7.10
g. Brake Over Travel Switch (BOTS) T.3.3
h. Inertia Switch T.9.4

input: What does rule EV.7.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Shutdown Circuit must directly carry the current driving the Accumulator Isolation Relays
(AIRs) and the Precharge Circuit Relay.

input: What does rule EV.7.1.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The AMS, IMD, and BSPD parts of the Shutdown Circuit must be Normally Open

input: What does rule EV.7.1.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The AMS, IMD and BSPD must have completely independent circuits to Open the Shutdown
Circuit.
The design of the respective circuits must ensure that a failure cannot result in electrical
power being fed back into the Shutdown Circuit.

input: What does rule EV.7.1.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Shutdown Buttons, BOTS, TSMS, GLVMS and Interlocks must directly carry the Shutdown
Circuit current

input: What does rule EV.7.1.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The team must be able to demonstrate all features and functions of the Shutdown Circuit and
components at Electrical Technical Inspection.

input: What does rule EV.7.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Shutdown Circuit Operation

input: What does rule EV.7.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Shutdown Circuit must Open when any of the following exist:
a. Operation of, or detection from any of the components listed in EV.7.1.1
b. Any shutdown of the GLV System

input: What does rule EV.7.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
When the Shutdown Circuit Opens:
a. The Tractive System must Shutdown
b. All Accumulator current flow must stop immediately EV.5.4.3

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 102 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
c. The voltage in the Tractive System must be Low Voltage T.9.1.2 in five seconds or less
d. The Motor(s) must spin free. Torque must not be applied to the Motor(s)

input: What does rule EV.7.2.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
When the AMS, IMD or BSPD Open the Shutdown Circuit:
a. The Tractive System must stay disabled until manually reset
b. The Tractive System must be reset only by manual action of a person directly at the
vehicle
c. The driver must not be able to reactivate the Tractive System from inside the vehicle
d. Operation of the Shutdown Buttons or TSMS must not reset the Shutdown Circuit

input: What does rule EV.7.2.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The driver may reset the Shutdown Circuit from the cockpit, subject to EV.7.2.3

input: What does rule EV.7.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Accumulator Management System - AMS

input: What does rule EV.7.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
An Accumulator Management System must monitor the Accumulator(s) Voltage EV.7.4 and
Temperature EV.7.5 when the:
a. Tractive System is Active EV.11.5
b. Accumulator is connected to a Charger EV.8.3

input: What does rule EV.7.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The AMS must have galvanic isolation at every segment to segment boundary, as approved in
the ESF

input: What does rule EV.7.3.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Cell balancing is not permitted when the Shutdown Circuit is Open ( EV.7.2, EV.8.4 )

input: What does rule EV.7.3.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The AMS must monitor for:
a. Voltage values outside the allowable range EV.7.4.2
b. Voltage sense Overcurrent Protection device(s) blown or tripped
c. Temperature values outside the allowable range EV.7.5.2
d. Missing or interrupted voltage or temperature measurements
e. A fault in the AMS

input: What does rule EV.7.3.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
If the AMS detects one or more of the conditions of EV.7.3.4 above, the AMS must:
a. Open the Shutdown Circuit EV.7.2.2
b. Turn on the AMS Indicator Light. The light must stay on until the AMS is reset EV.7.2.3

input: What does rule EV.7.3.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The AMS Indicator Light must be:
a. Color: Red
b. Clearly visible to the seated driver in bright sunlight
c. Clearly marked with the lettering “AMS”

input: What does rule EV.7.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Accumulator Voltage

input: What does rule EV.7.4.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The AMS must measure the cell voltage of every cell
When single cells are directly connected in parallel, only one voltage measurement is needed

input: What does rule EV.7.4.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Cell Voltage levels must stay inside the allowed minimum and maximum cell voltage levels
stated in the cell data sheet. Measurement accuracy must be considered.

input: What does rule EV.7.4.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All voltage sense wires to the AMS must meet one of:
a. Have Overcurrent Protection EV.7.4.4 below
b. Meet requirements for no Overcurrent Protection listed in EV.7.4.5 below

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 103 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule EV.7.4.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
When used, Overcurrent Protection for the AMS voltage sense wires must meet the following.
a. The Overcurrent Protection must occur in the conductor, wire or PCB trace which is
directly connected to the cell tab.
b. The voltage rating of the Overcurrent Protection must be equal to or higher than the
maximum segment voltage

input: What does rule EV.7.4.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Overcurrent Protection is not required on a voltage sense wire if all three of the following
conditions are met:
• AMS is a distributed AMS system (one cell measurement per board)
• Sense wire length is less than 25 mm
• AMS board has Overcurrent Protection

input: What does rule EV.7.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Accumulator Temperature

input: What does rule EV.7.5.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The AMS must measure the temperatures of critical points of the Accumulator

input: What does rule EV.7.5.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Temperatures (considering measurement accuracy) must stay below the lower of the two:
• The maximum cell temperature limit stated in the cell data sheet
• 60°C

input: What does rule EV.7.5.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Cell temperatures must be measured at the negative terminal of the respective cell

input: What does rule EV.7.5.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The temperature sensor used must be in direct contact with one of:
• The negative terminal itself
• The negative terminal busbar less than 10 mm away from the cell terminal

input: What does rule EV.7.5.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
For lithium based cells,
a. The temperature of a minimum of 20% of the cells must be monitored by the AMS
b. The monitored cells must be equally distributed inside the Accumulator Container(s)
Every cell temperature should be monitored

input: What does rule EV.7.5.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Multiple cells may be monitored with one temperature sensor, if EV.7.5 is met for all cells
sensed by the sensor.

input: What does rule EV.7.5.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Temperature sensors must have appropriate electrical isolation that meets one of the two:
• Between the sensor and cell
• In the sensing circuit
The isolation must consider both GLV/TS isolation as well as common mode voltages between
sense locations.

input: What does rule EV.7.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Insulation Monitoring Device - IMD

input: What does rule EV.7.6.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The vehicle must have an Insulation Monitoring Device (IMD) installed in the Tractive System

input: What does rule EV.7.6.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The IMD must be a Bender ISOMETER® IR155-3203 or IR155-3204 (website) or an approved
alternate equivalent IMD
Refer to the Rules FAQ on the FSAE Online website for approved equivalent IMD

input: What does rule EV.7.6.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The response value of the IMD must be set to 500 Ohm / Volt or higher, related to the
maximum Tractive System operation voltage.

input: What does rule EV.7.6.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The IMD must monitor the Tractive System for:
a. An isolation failure

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 104 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
b. A failure in the IMD operation
This must be done without the influence of any programmable logic.

input: What does rule EV.7.6.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
If the IMD detects one or more of the conditions of EV.7.6.4 above the IMD must:
a. Open the Shutdown Circuit EV.7.2.2
b. Turn on the IMD Indicator Light. The light must stay on until the IMD is reset EV.7.2.3

input: What does rule EV.7.6.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The IMD Indicator Light must be:
a. Color: Red
b. Clearly visible to the seated driver in bright sunlight
c. Clearly marked with the lettering “IMD”

input: What does rule EV.7.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Brake System Plausibility Device - BSPD

input: What does rule EV.7.7.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The vehicle must have a standalone nonprogrammable circuit to check for simultaneous
braking and high power output
The BSPD must be provided in addition to the APPS / Brake Pedal Plausibility Check (EV.4.7)

input: What does rule EV.7.7.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The BSPD must Open the Shutdown Circuit EV.7.2.2 when the two of these exist:
• Demand for Hard Braking EV.4.6
• Motor/Accumulator current is at a level where 5 kW of electrical power in the DC circuit
is delivered to the Motor(s) at the nominal battery voltage
The BSPD may delay opening the shutdown circuit up to 0.5 sec to avoid false trips

input: What does rule EV.7.7.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The BSPD must Open the Shutdown Circuit EV.7.2.2 when there is an open or short circuit in
any sensor input

input: What does rule EV.7.7.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The team must have a test to demonstrate BSPD operation at Electrical Technical Inspection.
a. Power must not be sent to the Motor(s) of the vehicle during the test
b. The test must prove the function of the complete BSPD in the vehicle, including the
current sensor
The suggested test would introduce a current by a separate wire from an external power
supply simulating the Tractive System current while pressing the brake pedal

input: What does rule EV.7.8 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Interlocks

input: What does rule EV.7.8.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Interlocks must be incorporated where specified (refer to EV.4.1.3, EV.5.5.2, EV.5.10 )

input: What does rule EV.7.8.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Additional Interlocks may be included in the Tractive System or components

input: What does rule EV.7.8.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Interlock is a wire or connection that must:
a. Open the Shutdown Circuit EV.7.2.2 if the Interlock connection is broken or interrupted
b. Not be in the low (ground) connection to the AIR coils of the Shutdown Circuit

input: What does rule EV.7.8.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Interlock circuits or connections do not require physical separation (EV.6.5) from Tractive
System wiring or components

input: What does rule EV.7.9 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Master Switches

input: What does rule EV.7.9.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Each vehicle must have two Master Switches that must:
a. Meet T.9.3 for Configuration and Location
b. Be direct acting, not act through a relay or logic

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 105 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule EV.7.9.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Grounded Low Voltage Master Switch (GLVMS) must:
a. Completely stop all power to the GLV System EV.4.4
b. Be in the center of a completely red circular area of > 50 mm in diameter
c. Be labeled “LV”

input: What does rule EV.7.9.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Tractive System Master Switch (TSMS) must:
a. Open the Shutdown Circuit in the OFF position EV.7.2.2
b. Be the last switch before the AIRs except for Precharge circuitry and Interlocks.
c. Be in the center of a completely orange circular area of > 50 mm in diameter
d. Be labeled “TS” and the symbol specified in ISO 7010-W012 (triangle with black
lightning bolt on yellow background).
e. Be fitted with a "lockout/tagout" capability in the OFF position EV.11.3.1

input: What does rule EV.7.10 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Shutdown Buttons
b. Adjustments to fit the driver IN.14.2.2
c. Tire changes per D.6.2

input: What does rule EV.7.10.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Three Shutdown Buttons must be installed on the vehicle.

input: What does rule EV.7.10.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Each Shutdown Button must be a push-pull or push-rotate emergency switch

input: What does rule EV.7.10.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
One Shutdown Button must be on each side of the vehicle which:
a. Is located aft of the driver’s compartment at approximately the level of the driver’s head
b. Has a diameter of 40 mm minimum
c. Must not be easily removable or mounted onto removable body work

input: What does rule EV.7.10.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
One Shutdown Button must be mounted in the cockpit which:
a. Is located in easy reach of the belted in driver, adjacent to the steering wheel, and
unobstructed by the steering wheel or any other part of the vehicle
b. Has diameter of 24 mm minimum

input: What does rule EV.7.10.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The international electrical symbol (a red spark on a white edged blue triangle) must be near
each Shutdown Button.

input: What does rule EV.7.10.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Pressing any of the Shutdown Buttons must Open the Shutdown Circuit EV.7.2.2

input: What does rule EV.8 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule EV.8.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Charger Requirements

input: What does rule EV.8.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All features and functions of the Charger and Charging Shutdown Circuit must be
demonstrated at Electrical Technical Inspection. IN.4.1

input: What does rule EV.8.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Chargers will be sealed after approval. IN.4.7.1

input: What does rule EV.8.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Charger Features

input: What does rule EV.8.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The Charger must be galvanically isolated (AC) input to (DC) output.

input: What does rule EV.8.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: If the Charger housing is conductive it must be connected to the earth ground of the AC input.

input: What does rule EV.8.2.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: All connections of the Charger(s) must be isolated and covered.

input: What does rule EV.8.2.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Charger connector(s) must incorporate a feature to let the connector become live only
when correctly connected to the Accumulator.

input: What does rule EV.8.2.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: High Voltage charging leads must be orange

input: What does rule EV.8.2.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Charger must have two TSMPs installed, see EV.5.8.2

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 106 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule EV.8.2.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Charger must include a Charger Shutdown Button which is:
a. A push-pull or push-rotate emergency switch
b. Minimum diameter of 25 mm
c. Labelled with the international electrical symbol (a red spark on a white edged blue
triangle)

input: What does rule EV.8.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Charging Shutdown Circuit

input: What does rule EV.8.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Charging Shutdown Circuit consists of:
a. Charger Shutdown Button EV.8.2.7
b. Accumulator Management System (AMS) EV.7.3
c. Insulation Monitoring Device (IMD) EV.7.6

input: What does rule EV.8.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The AMS and IMD parts of the Charging Shutdown Circuit must:
a. Be designed as Normally Open contacts
b. Have completely independent circuits to Open the Charging Shutdown Circuit.
Design of the respective circuits must ensure that a failure cannot result in electrical
power being fed back into the Charging Shutdown Circuit.

input: What does rule EV.8.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Charging Shutdown Circuit Operation

input: What does rule EV.8.4.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
When Charging, the AMS and IMD must:
a. Monitor the Accumulator
b. Open the Charging Shutdown Circuit if a fault is detected.

input: What does rule EV.8.4.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
When the Charging Shutdown Circuit Opens:
a. All current flow to the Accumulator must stop immediately
b. The voltage in the Tractive System must be Low Voltage T.9.1.2 in five seconds or less
c. The Charger must be turned off
d. The Charger must stay disabled until manually reset

input: What does rule EV.9 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule EV.9.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Activation Sequence
The vehicle systems must energize in the following sequence:
a. Low Voltage (GLV) System EV.9.2
b. Tractive System Active EV.9.3
c. Ready to Drive EV.9.4

input: What does rule EV.9.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Low Voltage (GLV) System
The Shutdown Circuit may be Closed when or after the GLV System is energized EV.7.2.4

input: What does rule EV.9.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Tractive System Active

input: What does rule EV.9.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Definition – High Voltage is present outside of the Accumulator Container

input: What does rule EV.9.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Tractive System Active must not be possible until both:
• GLV System is Energized
• Shutdown Circuit is Closed

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 107 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule EV.9.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Ready to Drive

input: What does rule EV.9.4.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Definition – the Motor(s) will respond to the input of the APPS

input: What does rule EV.9.4.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Ready to Drive must not be possible until the three at the same time:
• Tractive System Active EV.9.3
• The Brake Pedal is pressed and held to engage the mechanical brakes T.3.2
• The driver performs a manual action to initiate Ready to Drive
Such as pressing a specific button in the cockpit

input: What does rule EV.9.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Ready to Drive Sound

input: What does rule EV.9.5.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The vehicle must make a characteristic sound when it is Ready to Drive

input: What does rule EV.9.5.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Ready to Drive Sound must be:
a. Sounded continuously for minimum 1 second and maximum 3 seconds
b. A minimum sound level of 80 dBA, fast weighting IN.4.6
c. Easily recognizable. No animal voices, song parts or sounds that could be interpreted as
offensive will be accepted

input: What does rule EV.9.5.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The vehicle must not make other sounds similar to the Ready to Drive Sound.

input: What does rule EV.10 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule EV.10.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Onsite Registration

input: What does rule EV.10.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Accumulator must be onsite at the time the team registers to be eligible for Accumulator
Technical Inspection and Dynamic Events

input: What does rule EV.10.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Teams who register without the Accumulator:
a. Must not bring their Accumulator onsite for the duration of the competition
b. May participate in Technical Inspection and Static Events

input: What does rule EV.10.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Accumulator Removal

input: What does rule EV.10.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
After the team registers onsite, the Accumulator must remain on the competition site until
the end of the competition, or the team withdraws and leaves the site

input: What does rule EV.10.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Violators will be disqualified from the competition and must leave immediately

input: What does rule EV.11 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule EV.11.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Personnel

input: What does rule EV.11.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Electrical System Officer (ESO): AD.5.2
a. Is the only person on the team that may declare the vehicle electrically safe to allow
work on any system
b. Must accompany the vehicle when operated or moved at the competition site
c. Must be immediately available by phone at all times during the event

input: What does rule EV.11.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Maintenance

input: What does rule EV.11.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All participating team members must wear safety glasses with side shields at any time when:
a. Parts of the Tractive System are exposed while energized
b. Work is performed on the Accumulators

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 108 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule EV.11.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Appropriate insulated tools must be used when working on the Accumulator or Tractive
System

input: What does rule EV.11.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Lockout

input: What does rule EV.11.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The TSMS EV.7.9.3 must be locked in the OFF position when any work is done on the vehicle.

input: What does rule EV.11.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The HVD EV.5.5 must be disconnected when vehicles are:
a. Moved around the competition site
b. Participating in Static Events

input: What does rule EV.11.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Accumulator

input: What does rule EV.11.4.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The following work activities at competition are allowed only in the designated area and
during Electrical Technical Inspection IN.4 See EV.5.3.3
a. Opening Accumulator Containers
b. Any work on Accumulators, cells, or Segments
c. Energized electrical work

input: What does rule EV.11.4.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Accumulator cells and/or Accumulator Segment(s) must be moved at the competition site
inside one of the two:
a. Completely closed Accumulator Container EV.4.3 See EV.4.10.2
b. Segment/Cell Transport Container EV.11.4.3

input: What does rule EV.11.4.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Segment/Cell Transport Container(s) must be:
a. Electrically insulated
b. Protected from shock hazards and arc flash

input: What does rule EV.11.4.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Segments/Cells inside the Transport Container must agree with the voltage and energy limits
of EV.5.1.2

input: What does rule EV.11.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Charging

input: What does rule EV.11.5.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Accumulators must be removed from the vehicle inside the Accumulator Container and placed
on the Accumulator Container Hand Cart EV.4.10 for Charging.

input: What does rule EV.11.5.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Accumulator Charging must occur only inside the designated area

input: What does rule EV.11.5.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
A team member(s) who has knowledge of the Charging process must stay with the
Accumulator(s) during Charging

input: What does rule EV.11.5.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Each Accumulator Container(s) must have a label with the following data during Charging:
Team name and Electrical System Officer phone number(s)

input: What does rule EV.11.5.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Additional site specific rules or policies may apply

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 109 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
IN - TECHNICAL INSPECTION
The objective of Technical Inspection is to determine if the vehicle meets the Formula SAE
Rules requirements and restrictions and if, considered as a whole, it satisfies the intent of the
Rules.

input: What does rule F.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule F.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Chassis
The fabricated structural assembly that supports all functional vehicle systems.
This assembly may be a single fabricated structure, multiple fabricated structures or a
combination of composite and welded structures.

input: What does rule F.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Frame Member
A minimum representative single piece of uncut, continuous tubing.

input: What does rule F.1.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Monocoque
A type of Chassis where loads are supported by the external panels

input: What does rule F.1.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Main Hoop
A roll bar located alongside or immediately aft of the driver’s torso.

input: What does rule F.1.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Front Hoop
A roll bar located above the driver’s legs, in proximity to the steering wheel.

input: What does rule F.1.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Roll Hoop(s)
Referring to both the Front Hoop AND the Main Hoop

input: What does rule F.1.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Roll Hoop Bracing Supports
The structure from the lower end of the Roll Hoop Bracing back to the Roll Hoop(s).

input: What does rule F.1.8 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Front Bulkhead
A planar structure that provides protection for the driver’s feet.

input: What does rule F.1.9 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Impact Attenuator
A deformable, energy absorbing device located forward of the Front Bulkhead.

input: What does rule F.1.10 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Primary Structure
The combination of the following components:
• Front Bulkhead and Front Bulkhead Support
• Front Hoop, Main Hoop, Roll Hoop Braces and Supports
• Side Impact Structure
• (EV Only) Tractive System Protection and Rear Impact Protection
• Any Frame Members, guides, or supports that transfer load from the Driver Restraint
System

input: What does rule F.1.11 is about? return only the text of the rule

Primary Structure Envelope
A volume enclosed by multiple tangent planes, each of which follows the exact outline of the
Primary Structure Frame Members

input: What does rule F.1.12 is about? return only the text of the rule

Major Structure
The portion of the Chassis that lies inside the Primary Structure Envelope, excluding the Main
Hoop Bracing and the portion of the Main Hoop above a horizontal plane located at the top of
the Upper Side Impact Member or top of the Side Impact Zone.

The Primary Structure plus a plane from the top of the Main Hoop to the top of the Front
Hoop, plus a plane from the top of the Main Hoop to the rearmost Triangulated structural
tube, or monocoque equivalent.
* If there are no Triangulated Structural members aft of the Main Hoop, the Rollover
Protection Envelope ends at the rear plane of the Main Hoop

input: What does rule F.1.14 is about? return only the text of the rule

The volume enclosed by tangent lines between the Main Hoop and the outside edge of each

input: What does rule F.1.15 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Component Envelope
The area that is inside a plane from the top of the Main Hoop to the top of the Front
Bulkhead, plus a plane from the top of the Main Hoop to the rearmost Triangulated structural
tube, or monocoque equivalent. * see note in step F.1.13 above

input: What does rule F.1.16 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Buckling Modulus (EI)
Equal to E*I, where E = modulus of Elasticity, and I = area moment of inertia about the
weakest axis.

input: What does rule F.1.17 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Triangulation
An arrangement of Frame Members where all members and segments of members between
bends or nodes with Structural tubes form a structure composed entirely of triangles.
a. This is generally required between an upper member and a lower member, both of
which may have multiple segments requiring a diagonal to form multiple triangles.
b. This is also what is meant by “properly triangulated”.

input: What does rule F.3.4.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Properties for ANY steel material for calculations submitted in an SES must be:
a. Non Welded Properties for continuous material calculations:
Young’s Modulus (E) = 200 GPa (29,000 ksi)
Yield Strength (Sy) = 305 MPa (44.2 ksi)
Ultimate Strength (Su) = 365 MPa (52.9 ksi)
b. Welded Properties for discontinuous material such as joint calculations:
Yield Strength (Sy) = 180 MPa (26 ksi)
Ultimate Strength (Su) = 300 MPa (43.5 ksi)

input: What does rule F.3.4.3 is about? return only the text of the rule

input: What does rule F.3.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Alternative Tubing Materials

input: What does rule F.3.5.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Alternative Materials may be used for applications shown as permitted in F.3.2.1

input: What does rule F.3.5.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
If any Alternative Materials are used, the SES must contain:
a. Documentation of material type, (purchase receipt, shipping document or letter of
