output:
Accelerator Pedal
a. An Accelerator Pedal must control the Powertrain output
b. Pedal Travel is the percent of travel from a fully released position to a fully applied
position. 0% is fully released and 100% is fully applied.
c. The Accelerator Pedal must:
• Return to 0% Pedal Travel when not pushed
• Have a positive stop to prevent any cable, actuation system or sensor from damage
or overstress

input: What does rule T.1.4.2 is about? return only the text of the rule

This is to prevent debris or interference and includes but is not limited to a gear mechanism

input: What does rule T.1.4.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:

must be operated from inside the cockpit without any part of the driver, including hands, arms
or elbows, being outside of:
a. The Side Impact Structure defined in F.6.4 / F.7.5
b. Two longitudinal vertical planes parallel to the centerline of the chassis touching the
uppermost member of the Side Impact Structure

input: What does rule T.1.4.4 is about? return only the text of the rule

input: What does rule T.1.5.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Driver’s Seat must be protected by one of the following:
a. In side view, the lowest point of any Driver’s Seat must be no lower than the upper
surface of the lowest structural tube or equivalent
b. A longitudinal tube (or tubes) that meets the requirements for Side Impact tubing
(F.3.2.1.e), passing underneath the lowest point of the Driver Seat.

input: What does rule T.1.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Thermal Protection

input: What does rule T.1.6.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
When seated in the normal driving position, sufficient heat insulation must be provided to
ensure that the driver will not contact any metal or other materials which may become heated
to a surface temperature above 60°C.

input: What does rule T.1.6.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Insulation may be external to the cockpit or incorporated with the Driver’s Seat or Firewall.

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 57 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule T.1.6.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The design must address all three types of heat transfer between the heat source (examples
include but are not limited to: exhaust pipe, coolant hose/tube, Accumulator Container) and
the panel that the driver could contact (seat or floor):
a. Conduction Isolation by one of the following:
• No direct contact between the heat source and the panel
• A heat resistant, conduction isolation material with a minimum thickness of 8 mm
between the heat source and the panel
b. Convection Isolation by a minimum air gap of 25 mm between the heat source and the
panel
c. Radiation Isolation by one of the following:
• A solid metal heat shield with a minimum thickness of 0.4 mm
• Reflective foil or tape when combined with conduction insulation

input: What does rule T.1.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Floor Closeout

input: What does rule T.1.7.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: All vehicles must have a Floor Closeout to prevent track debris from entering

input: What does rule T.1.7.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The Floor Closeout must extend from the foot area to the firewall

input: What does rule T.1.7.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The panel(s) must be made of a solid, non brittle material

input: What does rule T.1.7.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: If multiple panels are used, gaps between panels must not exceed 3 mm

input: What does rule T.1.8 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Firewall(s)

input: What does rule T.1.8.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Requirement
A Firewall(s) must separate the driver compartment and any portion of the Driver Harness
from:
a. All components of the Fuel System, the engine oil, the liquid cooling systems, any lithium
batteries
b. (EV only) All Tractive System components other than Outboard Wheel Motors EV.4.1.3
where mounted at the wheels or on the front control arms

input: What does rule T.1.8.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Construction
Any Firewall must:
a. Be a non permeable surface made from a rigid, Nonflammable Material
b. Seal completely against the passage of fluids (the Firewall itself, edges, any pass throughs
and Floor Closeout)
c. Be rigidly mounted

input: What does rule T.1.8.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Positioning
The Firewall must extend sufficiently far upwards and/or rearwards and/or sideways where
any point on the drivers body less than 100 mm above the bottom of the helmet of the tallest
driver must not be in direct line of sight with any part given in T.1.8.1 above

input: What does rule T.1.8.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Details
a. Firewalls composed of multiple panels must overlap and be sealed at the joints
Sealing between firewalls must not be a stressed part of the Firewall
b. Grommets must be used to seal any pass through for wiring, cables, etc

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 58 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
c. Any seals or adhesives used with the Firewall must be rated for the application
environment

input: What does rule T.1.8.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: (EV only) The Accumulator Container must not be part of the Firewall

input: What does rule T.1.9 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Tractive System Firewalls (EV Only)

input: What does rule T.1.9.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Tractive System Firewalls must meet the requirements of T.1.8 above

input: What does rule T.1.9.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Tractive System Firewalls must be:
a. Made of aluminum
b. Grounded, refer to EV.6.7 Grounding

input: What does rule T.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule T.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Harness Definitions
a. 5 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts and one Anti-Submarine
Belt.
b. 6 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts and two leg or Anti-
Submarine Belts.
c. 7 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts, two leg or Anti-
Submarine Belts and a negative g or Z Belt.
d. Upright Driving Position - with a seat back angled at 30° or less from the vertical as
measured along the line joining the two 200 mm circles of the template of the 95th
percentile male as defined in F.5.6.4 and positioned per F.5.6.5
e. Reclined Driving Position - with a seat back angled at more than 30° from the vertical as
measured along the line joining the two 200 mm circles of the template of the 95th
percentile male as defined in F.5.6.4 and positioned per F.5.6.5
f. Chest to Groin Line - the straight line that in side view follows the line of the Shoulder
Belts from the chest to the release buckle.

input: What does rule T.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Harness Specification

input: What does rule T.2.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The vehicle must use a 5, 6 or 7 Point Harness meeting one or more of the following:
a. SFI Specification 16.1
b. SFI Specification 16.5
c. FIA specification 8853/2016

input: What does rule T.2.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The belts must have the original manufacturers labels showing the specification and
expiration date

input: What does rule T.2.2.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Harness must be in or before the year of expiration shown on the labels. Harnesses
expiring on or before Dec 31 of the calendar year of the competition are permitted.

input: What does rule T.2.2.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Harness must be in new or like new condition, with no signs of wear, cuts, chaffing or
other issues

input: What does rule T.2.2.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All Harness hardware must be installed and threaded in accordance with manufacturer’s
instructions

input: What does rule T.2.2.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All Harness hardware must be used as received from the manufacturer. No modification
(including drilling, cutting, grinding, etc) is permitted.

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 59 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule T.2.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Harness Requirements

input: What does rule T.2.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Vehicles with a Reclined Driving Position must have:
a. A 6 Point Harness or a 7 Point Harness
b. Anti-Submarine Belts with tilt lock adjusters (“quick adjusters”) OR two sets of Anti-
Submarine Belts installed.

input: What does rule T.2.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All Lap Belts must incorporate a tilt lock adjuster (“quick adjuster”).
Lap Belts with “pull-up” adjusters are recommended over “pull-down” adjusters.

input: What does rule T.2.3.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Shoulder Belts must be the over the shoulder type. Only separate shoulder straps are
permitted. “Y” type shoulder straps are not allowed. The “H” type configuration is allowed.

input: What does rule T.2.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Belt, Strap and Harness Installation - General

input: What does rule T.2.4.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Lap Belt, Shoulder Belts and Anti-Submarine Belt(s) must be securely mounted to the
Primary Structure.

input: What does rule T.2.4.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Any guide or support for the belts must be material meeting F.3.2.1.j

input: What does rule T.2.4.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Each tab or bracket to which any part of the Harness is attached must:
a. Support a minimum load in pullout and tearout before failure of:
• If one belt is attached to the tab or bracket 15 kN
• If two belts are attached to the tab or bracket 30 kN
b. Be 1.6 mm minimum thickness
c. Not be in bending when the attached part of the Harness is put under load
d. Not cause abrasion to the belt webbing

input: What does rule T.2.4.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Attachment of tabs or brackets must meet the following:
a. Where brackets are fastened to the chassis, no less than two 6 mm or 1/4” minimum
diameter Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2 or stronger must be used to attach the bracket to
the chassis.
b. Where a single shear tab is welded to the chassis, the tab to tube welding must be on
both sides of the base of the tab.
Double shear attachments are preferred. Tabs and brackets for double shear mounts should
be welded on both sides.

input: What does rule T.2.4.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Harness installation must meet T.1.8.1

input: What does rule T.2.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Lap Belt Mounting

input: What does rule T.2.5.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Lap Belts must pass around the pelvic area below the Anterior Superior Iliac Spines (the
hip bones)

input: What does rule T.2.5.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Installation of the Lap Belts must go in a straight line from the mounting point until they reach
the driver's body without touching any hole in the seat or any other intermediate structure

input: What does rule T.2.5.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The seat must be rolled or grommeted where the Belts or Harness pass through a hole in the
seat

input: What does rule T.2.5.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
With an Upright Driving Position:
a. The Lap Belt Side View Angle must be between 45° and 65° to the horizontal.
b. The centerline of the Lap Belt at the seat bottom should be between 0 – 75 mm forward
of the seat back to seat bottom junction.

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 60 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule T.2.5.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
With a Reclined Driving Position, the Lap Belt Side View Angle must be between 60° and 80° to
the horizontal.

input: What does rule T.2.5.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Lap Belts must attach by one of the two:
a. Bolt or eyebolt through a welded tube insert or tested monocoque attachment F.7.9
b. Bolt or clip to a tab or bracket ( T.2.4.3 ) on a tube frame

input: What does rule T.2.5.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
In side view, the Lap Belt must be capable of pivoting freely by using a shouldered bolt or an
eye bolt attachment

input: What does rule T.2.5.8 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Any bolt used to attach a Lap Belt, directly to the chassis or to an intermediate bracket, is a
Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2, with a minimum diameter that is the smaller of:
• The bolt diameter specified by the manufacturer
• 10 mm or 3/8”

input: What does rule T.2.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Shoulder Harness

input: What does rule T.2.6.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
From the driver’s shoulders rearwards to the mounting point or structural guide, the Shoulder
Belt Side View Angle must be between 10° above the horizontal and 20° below the horizontal.

input: What does rule T.2.6.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The Shoulder Belt Mount Spacing must be between 175 mm and 235 mm, center to center

input: What does rule T.2.6.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Shoulder Belts must attach by one of the four:
a. Wrap around the Shoulder Harness Mounting bar
b. Bolt through a welded tube insert or tested monocoque attachment F.7.9
c. Bolt or clip to a tab or bracket ( T.2.4.3 ) on the Shoulder Harness Mounting bar
d. Wrap around physically tested hardware attached to a monocoque

input: What does rule T.2.6.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Any bolt used to attach a Shoulder Belt, directly to the chassis or to an intermediate bracket, is
a Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2, with a minimum diameter that is the smaller of:
• The bolt diameter specified by the manufacturer
• 10 mm or 3/8”

input: What does rule T.2.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Anti-Submarine Belt Mounting

input: What does rule T.2.7.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Anti-Submarine Belt of a 5 point harness must be mounted with the mounting point in
line with or slightly forward of the driver’s Chest to Groin Line with an Anti-Submarine Belt
Side View Angle no more than 20°

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 61 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule T.2.7.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Anti-Submarine Belts of a 6 point harness must be mounted in one of the following ways:
a. With the belts going vertically down from the groin, or with an Anti-Submarine Belt Side
View Angle up to 20° rearwards. The Anti-Submarine Belt Mount Spacing should be
approximately 100 mm apart.

b. With the Anti-Submarine Belt Mounting Points on the Primary Structure at or near the
Lap Belt anchorages, the driver sitting on the Anti-Submarine Belts, and the belts coming
up around the groin to the release buckle.

input: What does rule T.2.7.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Installation of all Anti-Submarine Belts must go in a straight line from the Anti-Submarine Belt
Mounting Point(s) without touching any hole in the seat or any other intermediate structure
until they reach:
a. The release buckle for the 5 Point Harness mounting per T.2.7.1
b. The first point where the belt touches the driver’s body for the 6 Point Harness mounting
per T.2.7.2

input: What does rule T.2.7.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Anti Submarine Belts must attach by one of the three:
a. Bolt or eyebolt through a welded tube insert or tested monocoque attachment F.7.9
b. Bolt or clip to a tab or bracket ( T.2.4.3 ) on a tube frame

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 62 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
c. Wrap around a tube meeting F.3.2.1.j that connects the Lower Side Impact tubes F.6.4.5.
The belt must not be able to touch the ground.

input: What does rule T.2.7.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Any bolt used to attach an Anti-Submarine Belt, directly to the chassis or to an intermediate
bracket, is a Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2, with a minimum diameter that is the smaller of:
• The bolt diameter specified by the manufacturer
• 8 mm or 5/16”

input: What does rule T.2.8 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Head Restraint

input: What does rule T.2.8.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: A Head Restraint must be provided to limit the rearward motion of the driver’s head.

input: What does rule T.2.8.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The Head Restraint must be vertical or near vertical in side view.

input: What does rule T.2.8.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All material and structure of the Head Restraint must be inside one or both of:
a. Rollover Protection Envelope F.1.13
b. Head Restraint Protection (if used) F.5.10

input: What does rule T.2.8.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Head Restraint, attachment and mounting must be strong enough to withstand a
minimum force of:
a. 900 N applied in a rearward direction
b. 300 N applied in a lateral or vertical direction

input: What does rule T.2.8.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
For all drivers, the Head Restraint must be located and adjusted where:
a. The Head Restraint is no more than 25 mm away from the back of the driver’s helmet,
with the driver in their normal driving position.
b. The contact point of the back of the driver’s helmet on the Head Restraint is no less than
50 mm from any edge of the Head Restraint.
Approximately 100 mm of longitudinal adjustment should accommodate range of specified
drivers. Several Head Restraints with different thicknesses may be used

input: What does rule T.2.8.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Head Restraint padding must:
a. Be an energy absorbing material that is one of the two:
• Meets SFI Spec 45.2
• CONFOR CF45 (Blue) or CONFOR CF45M (Blue) FIA Technical List No 17
b. Have a minimum thickness of 38 mm
c. Have a minimum width of 15 cm
d. Meet one of the following:
• minimum area of 235 cm2 AND minimum total height adjustment of 17.5 cm
• minimum height of 28 cm
e. Be covered with a thin, flexible material that contains a ~20 mm diameter inspection
hole in a surface other than the front surface

input: What does rule T.2.9 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Roll Bar Padding
Any portion of the roll bar, roll bar bracing or Chassis which might be contacted by the driver’s
helmet must be covered with a minimum thickness of 12 mm of padding which meets SFI Spec
45.1 or FIA 8857-2001.

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 63 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule T.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule T.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Brake System

input: What does rule T.3.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The vehicle must have a Brake System

input: What does rule T.3.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Brake System must:
a. Act on all four wheels
b. Be operated by a single control
c. Be capable of locking all four wheels

input: What does rule T.3.1.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Brake System must have two independent hydraulic circuits
A leak or failure at any point in the Brake System must maintain effective brake power on
minimum two wheels

input: What does rule T.3.1.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Each hydraulic circuit must have its own fluid reserve using separate reservoirs or an OEM
style reservoir

input: What does rule T.3.1.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: A single brake acting on a limited slip differential may be used

input: What does rule T.3.1.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: “Brake by Wire” systems are prohibited

input: What does rule T.3.1.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Unarmored plastic brake lines are prohibited

input: What does rule T.3.1.8 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Brake System must be protected with scatter shields from failure of the drive train (see
, control, pressurized, electrical components are located below

input: What does rule T.3.1.9 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
In side view any portion of the Brake System that is mounted on the sprung part of the vehicle
must not project below the lower surface of the chassis

input: What does rule T.3.1.10 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Fasteners in the Brake System are Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2

input: What does rule T.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
, T.6.1.7, EV.10, EV.11, D.12.13, D.13.4
Selected changes: V.1.4.2, F.5.7.7, F.6.5.3, F.7.1.4, T.5.2.2, T.5.4.3, T.9.2.1, EV.4.4.1, EV.5.3.2.b,
Brake Pedal, Pedal Box and Mounting

input: What does rule T.3.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Brake Pedal must be one of:
• Fabricated from steel or aluminum
• Machined from steel, aluminum or titanium

input: What does rule T.3.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Brake Pedal and associated components design must withstand a minimum force of 2000
N without any failure of the Brake System, pedal box, chassis mounting, or pedal adjustment
This is not a design criteria. The Brake Pedal and Brake System may be tested by pressing the
pedal with the maximum force that can be exerted by any official when seated normally

input: What does rule T.3.2.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Failure of non-loadbearing components in the Brake System or pedal box must not interfere
with Brake Pedal operation or Brake System function

input: What does rule T.3.2.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
(EV only) Additional requirements for Electric Vehicles:
a. The first 90% of the Brake Pedal travel may be used to regenerate energy without
actuating the hydraulic brake system.
b. The remaining Brake Pedal travel must directly actuate the hydraulic brake system.
Brake energy regeneration may stay active.

input: What does rule T.3.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Brake Over Travel Switch - BOTS

input: What does rule T.3.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The vehicle must have a Brake Over Travel Switch (BOTS). Brake pedal travel exceeding the
normal range will actuate the switch

input: What does rule T.3.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The BOTS must be a mechanical single pole, single throw (commonly known as a two position)
switch (push-pull or flip type).

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 64 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule T.3.3.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Operation of the BOTS to the OFF position must Open the Shutdown Circuit IC.9.2.2 / EV.7.2.2

input: What does rule T.3.3.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Repeated operation of the switch must not reset or restore power

input: What does rule T.3.3.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The driver must not be able to reset the BOTS.

input: What does rule T.3.3.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The BOTS must be implemented with analog components, and not using programmable logic
controllers, engine control units, or similar functioning digital controllers.

input: What does rule T.3.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Brake Light

input: What does rule T.3.4.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The vehicle must have a Brake Light that is clearly visible from the rear in very bright sunlight.

input: What does rule T.3.4.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Brake Light must be:
a. Red in color on a Black background
b. Rectangular, triangular or near round shape with a minimum shining surface of 15 cm2
c. Mounted between the wheel centerline and driver’s shoulder level vertically and
approximately on vehicle centerline laterally.

input: What does rule T.3.4.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: When LED lights are used without a diffuser, they must not be more than 20 mm apart.

input: What does rule T.3.4.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: If a single line of LEDs is used, the minimum length is 150 mm.

input: What does rule T.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule T.4.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Applicability
This section T.4 applies only for:
• IC vehicles using Electronic Throttle Control (ETC) IC.4
• EV vehicles

input: What does rule T.4.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Accelerator Pedal Position Sensor - APPS

input: What does rule T.4.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Accelerator Pedal must actuate the APPS T.1.4.1
a. Two springs must be used to return the foot pedal to 0% Pedal Travel
b. Each spring must be capable of returning the pedal to 0% Pedal Travel with the other
disconnected. The springs in the APPS are not acceptable pedal return springs.

input: What does rule T.4.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Two or more electrically separate sensors must be used as APPSs. A single OEM type APPS
with two completely separate sensors in a single housing is acceptable.

input: What does rule T.4.2.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The APPS sensors must have different transfer functions which meet one of the two:
• Each sensor has different gradients and/or offsets to the other(s). The circuit must have
a pull-up or pull-down resistor to bring an open circuit input to 0% Pedal Travel
• An OEM pedal sensor with opposite slopes. Non OEM opposite slope sensor
configurations require prior approval.
The intent is that in a short circuit the APPSs will only agree at 0% Pedal Travel

input: What does rule T.4.2.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Implausibility is defined as a deviation of more than 10% Pedal Travel between the sensors or
other failure as defined in this Section T.4.2. Use of values larger than 10% Pedal Travel
require justification in the ETC Systems Form and may not be approved

input: What does rule T.4.2.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
If an Implausibility occurs between the values of the APPSs and persists for more than 100
msec, the power to the (IC) Electronic Throttle / (EV) Motor(s) must be immediately stopped
completely.

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 65 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
(EV only) It is not necessary to Open the Shutdown Circuit, the motor controller(s) stopping
the power to the Motor(s) is sufficient.

input: What does rule T.4.2.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
If three sensors are used, then in the case of an APPS failure, any two sensors that agree
within 10% Pedal Travel may be used to define the (IC) throttle position / (EV) torque target
and the 3rd APPS may be ignored.

input: What does rule T.4.2.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Each APPS must be able to be checked during Technical Inspection by having one of the two:
• A separate detachable connector that enables a check of functions by unplugging it
• An inline switchable breakout box available that allows disconnection of each APPS
signal.

input: What does rule T.4.2.8 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The APPS signals must be sent directly to a controller using an analogue signal or via a digital
data transmission bus such as CAN or FlexRay.

input: What does rule T.4.2.9 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Any failure of the APPS or APPS wiring must be detectable by the controller and must be
treated like an Implausibility, see T.4.2.4 above

input: What does rule T.4.2.10 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
When an analogue signal is used, the APPS will be considered to have failed when they
achieve an open circuit or short circuit condition which generates a signal outside of the
normal operating range, for example <0.5 V or >4.5 V.
The circuitry used to evaluate the sensor must use pull down or pull up resistors to ensure
that open circuit signals result in a failure being detected.

input: What does rule T.4.2.11 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
When any kind of digital data transmission is used to transmit the APPS signal,
a. The ETC Systems Form must contain a detailed description of all the potential failure
modes that can occur, the strategy that is used to detect these failures and the tests that
have been conducted to prove that the detection strategy works.
b. The failures to be considered must include but are not limited to the failure of the APPS,
APPS signals being out of range, corruption of the message and loss of messages and the
associated time outs.

input: What does rule T.4.2.12 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The current rules are written to only apply to the APPS (pedal), but the integrity of the torque
command signal is important in all stages.

input: What does rule T.4.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Brake System Encoder - BSE

input: What does rule T.4.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The vehicle must have a sensor or switch to measure brake pedal position or brake system
pressure

input: What does rule T.4.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The BSE must be able to be checked during Technical Inspection by having one of:
• A separate detachable connector(s) for any BSE signal(s) to the main ECU without
affecting any other connections
• An inline switchable breakout box available that allows disconnection of each BSE
signal(s) to the main ECU without affecting any other connections

input: What does rule T.4.3.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The BSE or switch signals must be sent directly to a controller using an analogue signal or via a
digital data transmission bus such as CAN or FlexRay
Any failure of the BSE or BSE wiring that persists more than 100 msec must be detectable by
the controller and treated like an implausibility and power to the (IC) electronic throttle / (EV)
Motor(s) must be immediately stopped completely.
(EV only) It is not necessary to completely deactivate the Tractive System, the motor
controller(s) stopping power to the motor(s) is sufficient.

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 66 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule T.4.3.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
When an analogue signal is used, the BSE sensors will be considered to have failed when they
achieve an open circuit or short circuit condition which generates a signal outside of the
normal operating range, for example <0.5 V or >4.5 V.
The circuitry used to evaluate the sensor must use pull down or pull up resistors to ensure
that open circuit signals result in a failure being detected.

input: What does rule T.4.3.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
When any kind of digital data transmission is used to transmit the BSE signal:
a. The ETC Systems Form must contain a detailed description of all the potential failure
modes that can occur, the strategy that is used to detect these failures and the tests that
have been conducted to prove that the detection strategy works.
b. The failures modes must include but are not limited to the failure of the sensor, sensor
signals being out of range, corruption of the message and loss of messages and the
associated time outs.
c. In all cases a sensor failure must immediately shutdown power to the motor(s).

input: What does rule T.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule T.5.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Transmission and Drive
Any transmission and drivetrain may be used.

input: What does rule T.5.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
) or from minor collisions.
Drivetrain Shields and Guards

input: What does rule T.5.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Exposed high speed final drivetrain equipment such as Continuously Variable Transmissions
(CVTs), sprockets, gears, pulleys, torque converters, clutches, belt drives, clutch drives and
electric motors, must be fitted with scatter shields intended to contain drivetrain parts in case
of radial failure

input: What does rule T.5.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The final drivetrain shield must:
a. Be made with solid material (not perforated)
b. Cover the chain or belt from the drive sprocket to the driven sprocket/chain wheel/belt
or pulley
c. Start and end no higher than parallel to the lowest point of the chain wheel/belt/pulley:

d. Cover the bottom of the chain or belt or rotating component when fuel, brake lines

input: What does rule T.5.2.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Body panels or other existing covers are acceptable when constructed per T.5.2.7 / T.5.2.8

input: What does rule T.5.2.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Frame Members or existing components that exceed the scatter shield material requirements
may be used as part of the shield.

input: What does rule T.5.2.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Scatter shields may be composed of multiple pieces. Any gaps must be small (< 3 mm)

input: What does rule T.5.2.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: If equipped, the engine drive sprocket cover may be used as part of the scatter shield system.

input: What does rule T.5.2.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Chain Drive - Scatter shields for chains must:
a. Be made of 2.66 mm (0.105 inch) minimum thickness steel (no alternatives are allowed)

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 67 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
b. Have a minimum width equal to three times the width of the chain
c. Be centered on the center line of the chain
d. Stay aligned with the chain under all conditions

input: What does rule T.5.2.8 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Non-metallic Belt Drive - Scatter shields for belts must:
a. Be made from 3.0 mm minimum thickness aluminum alloy 6061-T6
b. Have a minimum width that is equal to 1.7 times the width of the belt.
c. Be centered on the center line of the belt
d. Stay aligned with the belt under all conditions

input: What does rule T.5.2.9 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Attachment Fasteners - All fasteners attaching scatter shields and guards must be 6 mm or
1/4” minimum diameter Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2

input: What does rule T.5.2.10 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Finger Guards
a. Must cover any drivetrain parts that spin while the vehicle is stationary with the engine
running.
b. Must be made of material sufficient to resist finger forces.
c. Mesh or perforated material may be used but must prevent the passage of a 12 mm
diameter object through the guard.

input: What does rule T.5.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Motor Protection (EV Only)

input: What does rule T.5.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The rotating part of the Motor(s) EV.4.1 must be contained in a structural casing.
The motor casing may be the original motor casing, a team built motor casing or the original
casing with additional material added to achieve the minimum required thickness.
• Minimum thickness for aluminum alloy 6061-T6: 3.0 mm
If lower grade aluminum alloy is used, then the material must be thicker to provide an
equivalent strength.
• Minimum thickness for steel: 2.0 mm

input: What does rule T.5.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
A Scatter Shield must be included around the Motor(s) when one or both:
• The motor casing rotates around the stator
• The motor case is perforated

input: What does rule T.5.3.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Motor Scatter Shield must be:
• Made from aluminum alloy 6061-T6 or steel
• Minimum thickness: 1.0 mm

input: What does rule T.5.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Coolant Fluid

input: What does rule T.5.4.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Water cooled engines must use only plain water with no additives of any kind

input: What does rule T.5.4.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Liquid coolant for electric motors, Accumulators or HV electronics must be one of:
• plain water with no additives
• oil

input: What does rule T.5.4.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: (EV only) Liquid coolant must not directly touch the cells in the Accumulator

input: What does rule T.5.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: System Sealing

input: What does rule T.5.5.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Any cooling or lubrication system must be sealed to prevent leakage

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 68 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule T.5.5.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The vehicle must be capable of being tilted to a 45° angle without leaking fluid of any type.

input: What does rule T.5.5.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Flammable liquid and vapors or other leaks must not collect or contact the driver

input: What does rule T.5.5.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Two holes of minimum diameter 25 mm each must be provided in the structure or belly pan at
the locations:
a. The lowest point of the chassis
b. Rearward of the driver position, forward of a fuel tank or other liquid source
c. If the lowest point of the chassis obeys T.5.5.4.b, then only one set of holes T.5.5.4.a is
necessary

input: What does rule T.5.5.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Absorbent material and open collection devices (regardless of material) are prohibited in
compartments containing engine, drivetrain, exhaust and fuel systems below the highest
point on the exhaust system.

input: What does rule T.5.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Catch Cans

input: What does rule T.5.6.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The vehicle must have separate containers (catch cans) to retain fluids from any vents from
the powertrain systems.

input: What does rule T.5.6.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Catch cans must be:
a. Capable of containing boiling water without deformation
b. Located rearwards of the Firewall below the driver’s shoulder level
c. Positively retained, using no tie wraps or tape

input: What does rule T.5.6.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Catch cans for the engine coolant system and engine lubrication system must have a minimum
capacity of 10% of the fluid being contained or 0.9 liter, whichever is higher

input: What does rule T.5.6.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Catch cans for any vent on other systems containing liquid lubricant or coolant, including a
differential, gearbox, or electric motor, must have a minimum capacity of 10% of the fluid
being contained or 0.5 liter, whichever is higher

input: What does rule T.5.6.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Any catch can on the cooling system must vent through a hose with a minimum internal
diameter of 3 mm down to the bottom levels of the Chassis.

input: What does rule T.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule T.6.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Compressed Gas Cylinders and Lines
Any system on the vehicle that uses a compressed gas as an actuating medium must meet the
following:

input: What does rule T.6.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Working Gas - The working gas must be non flammable

input: What does rule T.6.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Cylinder Certification - The gas cylinder/tank must be commercially manufactured, designed
and built for the pressure being used, certified by an accredited testing laboratory in the
country of its origin, and labeled or stamped appropriately.

input: What does rule T.6.1.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Pressure Regulation - The pressure regulator must be mounted directly onto the gas
cylinder/tank

input: What does rule T.6.1.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Lines and Fittings - The gas lines and fittings must be appropriate for the maximum possible
operating pressure of the system.

input: What does rule T.6.1.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Insulation - The gas cylinder/tank must be insulated from any heat sources

input: What does rule T.6.1.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Cylinder Material – gas cylinders/tanks in a position 150 mm or less from an exhaust system
must meet one of the two:
• Made from metal

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 69 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
• Meet the thermal protection requirements of T.1.6.3

input: What does rule T.6.1.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Cylinder Location - The gas cylinder/tank and the pressure regulator must be:
a. Securely mounted inside the Chassis
b. Located outside of the Cockpit
c. In a position below the height of the Shoulder Belt Mount T.2.6
d. Aligned so the axis of the gas cylinder/tank does not point at the driver

input: What does rule T.6.1.8 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Protection – The gas cylinder/tank and lines must be protected from rollover, collision from
any direction, or damage resulting from the failure of rotating equipment

input: What does rule T.6.1.9 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The driver must be protected from failure of the cylinder/tank and regulator

input: What does rule T.6.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
High Pressure Hydraulic Pumps and Lines
This section T.6.2 does not apply to Brake lines or hydraulic clutch lines

input: What does rule T.6.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The driver and anyone standing outside the vehicle must be shielded from any hydraulic
pumps and lines with line pressures of 2100 kPa or higher.

input: What does rule T.6.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The shields must be steel or aluminum with a minimum thickness of 1 mm.

input: What does rule T.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule T.7.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Aerodynamic Devices

input: What does rule T.7.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Aerodynamic Device
A part on the vehicle which guides airflow for purposes including generation of downforce
and/or change of drag.
Examples include but are not limited to: wings, undertray, splitter, endplates, vanes

input: What does rule T.7.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
No power device may be used to move or remove air from under the vehicle. Power ground
effects are strictly prohibited.

input: What does rule T.7.1.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All Aerodynamic Devices must meet:
a. The mounting system provides sufficient rigidity in the static condition
b. The Aerodynamic Devices do not oscillate or move excessively when the vehicle is
moving. Refer to IN.8.2

input: What does rule T.7.1.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All forward facing edges that could contact a pedestrian (wings, end plates, and undertrays)
must have a minimum radius of 5 mm for all horizontal edges and 3 mm for vertical edges.
This may be the radius of the edges themselves, or additional permanently attached pieces
designed to meet this requirement.

input: What does rule T.7.1.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Other edges that a person may touch must not be sharp

input: What does rule T.7.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Bodywork

input: What does rule T.7.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Conventionally designed Bodywork or a nose cone is not considered an Aerodynamic Device

input: What does rule T.7.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Bodywork, a nose cone, or another component mounted to the vehicle is an Aerodynamic
Device if is designed to, or may possibly, produce force due to aerodynamic effects

input: What does rule T.7.2.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Bodywork must not contain openings into the cockpit from the front of the vehicle back to the
Main Hoop or Firewall. The cockpit opening and minimal openings around the front
suspension components are allowed.

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 70 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule T.7.2.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All forward facing edges on the Bodywork that could contact people, including the nose, must
have forward facing radii minimum 38 mm. This minimum radius must extend 45° or more
relative to the forward direction, along the top, sides and bottom of all affected edges.

input: What does rule T.7.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Measurement

input: What does rule T.7.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All Aerodynamic Device limitations are measured:
a. With the wheels pointing in the straight ahead position
b. Without a driver in the vehicle
The intent is to standardize the measurement, see GR.6.4.1

input: What does rule T.7.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Head Restraint Plane
A transverse vertical plane through the rearmost portion of the front face of the driver head
restraint support, excluding any padding, set (if adjustable) in its fully rearward position

input: What does rule T.7.3.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Rear Aerodynamic Zone
The volume that is:
• Rearward of the Head Restraint Plane
• Inboard of two vertical planes parallel to the centerline of the chassis touching the inside
of the rear tires at the height of the hub centerline

input: What does rule T.7.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Location
Any part of any Aerodynamic Device or Bodywork must meet V.1.1 and V.1.4.1

input: What does rule T.7.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Length
In plan view, any part of any Aerodynamic Device must be:
a. No more than 700 mm forward of the fronts of the front tires
b. No more than 250 mm rearward of the rear of the rear tires

input: What does rule T.7.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Width
In plan view, any part of any Aerodynamic Device must be:

input: What does rule T.7.6.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
When forward of the centerline of the front wheel axles:
Inboard of two vertical planes parallel to the centerline of the chassis touching the outside of
the front tires at the height of the hubs.

input: What does rule T.7.6.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
When between the centerlines of the front and rear wheel axles:
Inboard of a line drawn connecting the outer surfaces of the front and rear tires at the height
of the wheel centers

input: What does rule T.7.6.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
When rearward of the centerline of the rear wheel axles:
In the Rear Aerodynamic Zone

input: What does rule T.7.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Height

input: What does rule T.7.7.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Any part of any Aerodynamic Device that is located:
a. In the Rear Aerodynamic Zone must be no higher than 1200 mm above the ground
b. Outside of the Rear Aerodynamic Zone must be no higher than 500 mm above the
ground

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 71 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
c. Forward of the centerline of the front wheel axles and outboard of two vertical planes
parallel to the centerline of the chassis touching the inside of the front tires at the height
of the hubs must be no higher than 250 mm above the ground

input: What does rule T.7.7.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Bodywork height is not restricted when the Bodywork is located:
• Between the transverse vertical planes positioned at the front and rear axle centerlines
• Inside two vertical fore and aft planes 400 mm outboard from the centerline on each
side of the vehicle

input: What does rule T.8 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule T.8.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Critical Fasteners
A fastener (bolt, screw, pin, etc) used in a location specified in the applicable rule

input: What does rule T.8.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
. Additional requirements apply in F.5.12.5 and F.5.12.7
b. No spherical rod ends are allowed.
c. The attachment holes in the lugs, the attached bracing and the sleeves and tubes must
be a close fit with the pin or bolt.
Critical Fastener Requirements

input: What does rule T.8.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Any Critical Fastener must meet, at minimum, one of the following:
a. SAE Grade 5
b. Metric Class 8.8
c. AN/MS Specifications
d. Equivalent to or better than above, as approved by a Rules Question or at Technical
Inspection

input: What does rule T.8.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All threaded Critical Fasteners must be one of the following:
• Hex head
• Hexagonal recessed drive (Socket Head Cap Screws or Allen screws/bolts)

input: What does rule T.8.2.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All Critical Fasteners must be secured from unintentional loosening with Positive Locking
Mechanisms see T.8.3

input: What does rule T.8.2.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: A minimum of two full threads must project from any lock nut.

input: What does rule T.8.2.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Some Critical Fastener applications have additional requirements that are provided in the
applicable section.

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 72 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule T.8.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Positive Locking Mechanisms

input: What does rule T.8.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Positive Locking Mechanisms are defined as those which:
a. Technical Inspectors / team members can see that the device/system is in place (visible).
b. Do not rely on the clamping force to apply the locking or anti vibration feature.
Meaning If the fastener begins to loosen, the locking device still prevents the fastener coming
completely loose

input: What does rule T.8.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Examples of acceptable Positive Locking Mechanisms include, but are not limited to:
a. Correctly installed safety wiring
b. Cotter pins
c. Nylon lock nuts (where temperature does not exceed 80°C)
d. Prevailing torque lock nuts
Lock washers, bolts with nylon patches and thread locking compounds (Loctite®), DO NOT
meet the positive locking requirement.

input: What does rule T.8.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Requirements for All Fasteners
Adjustable tie rod ends must be constrained with a jam nut to prevent loosening.

input: What does rule T.9 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule T.9.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Definitions

input: What does rule T.9.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
High Voltage – HV
Any voltage more than 60 V DC or 25 V AC RMS

input: What does rule T.9.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Low Voltage - LV
Any voltage less than and including 60 V DC or 25 V AC RMS

input: What does rule T.9.1.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Normally Open
A type of electrical relay or contactor that allows current flow only in the energized state

input: What does rule T.9.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Low Voltage Batteries

input: What does rule T.9.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All Low Voltage Batteries and onboard power supplies must be securely mounted inside the
Chassis below the height of the Shoulder Belt Mount T.2.6

input: What does rule T.9.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All Low Voltage batteries must have Overcurrent Protection that trips at or below the
maximum specified discharge current of the cells

input: What does rule T.9.2.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The hot (ungrounded) terminal must be insulated.

input: What does rule T.9.2.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Any wet cell battery located in the driver compartment must be enclosed in a nonconductive
marine type container or equivalent.

input: What does rule T.9.2.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Batteries or battery packs based on lithium chemistry must meet one of the two:
a. Have a rigid, sturdy casing made from Nonflammable Material
b. A commercially available battery designed as an OEM style replacement

input: What does rule T.9.2.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All batteries using chemistries other than lead acid must be presented at Technical Inspection
with markings identifying it for comparison to a datasheet or other documentation proving
the pack and supporting electronics meet all rules requirements

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 73 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule T.9.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Master Switches
Each Master Switch ( IC.9.3 / EV.7.9 ) must meet the following:

input: What does rule T.9.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Location
a. On the driver’s right hand side of the vehicle
b. In proximity to the Main Hoop
c. At the driver's shoulder height
d. Able to be easily actuated from outside the vehicle

input: What does rule T.9.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Characteristics
a. Be of the rotary mechanical type
b. Be rigidly mounted to the vehicle and must not be removed during maintenance
c. Mounted where the rotary axis of the key is near horizontal and across the vehicle
d. The ON position must be in the horizontal position and must be marked accordingly
e. The OFF position must be clearly marked
f. (EV Only) Operated with a red removable key that must only be removable in the
electrically open position

input: What does rule T.9.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Inertia Switch

input: What does rule T.9.4.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Inertia Switch Requirement
• (EV) Must have an Inertia Switch
• (IC) Should have an Inertia Switch

input: What does rule T.9.4.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Inertia Switch must be:
a. A Sensata Resettable Crash Sensor or equivalent
b. Mechanically and rigidly attached to the vehicle
c. Removable to test functionality

input: What does rule T.9.4.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Inertia Switch operation:
a. Must trigger due to a longitudinal impact load which decelerates the vehicle at between
8 g and 11 g depending on the duration of the deceleration (refer to spec sheet of the
Sensata device)
b. Must Open the Shutdown Circuit IC.9.2.2 / EV.7.2.2 if triggered
c. Must latch until manually reset
d. May be reset by the driver from inside the driver's cell

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 74 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
VE - VEHICLE AND DRIVER EQUIPMENT

input: What does rule V.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Configuration ..................................................................................................................................... 19
CONFIGURATION
The vehicle must be open wheeled and open cockpit (a formula style body) with four wheels
that are not in a straight line.

input: What does rule V.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Open Wheel
Open Wheel vehicles must satisfy all of the following criteria:
a. The top 180° of the wheels/tires must be unobstructed when viewed from vertically
above the wheel.
b. The wheels/tires must be unobstructed when viewed from the side.
c. No part of the vehicle may enter a keep out zone defined by two lines extending
vertically from positions 75 mm in front of and 75 mm aft of, the outer diameter of the
front and rear tires in the side view elevation of the vehicle, with tires steered straight
ahead. This keep out zone will extend laterally from the outside plane of the wheel/tire
to the inboard plane of the wheel/tire.

input: What does rule V.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Wheelbase
The vehicle must have a minimum wheelbase of 1525 mm

input: What does rule V.1.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Vehicle Track

input: What does rule V.1.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The track and center of gravity must combine to provide sufficient rollover stability. See

input: What does rule V.1.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The smaller track of the vehicle (front or rear) must be no less than 75% of the larger track.

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 20 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule V.1.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Ground Clearance

input: What does rule V.1.4.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Ground clearance must be sufficient to prevent any portion of the vehicle except the tires
from touching the ground during dynamic events

input: What does rule V.1.4.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The distance to the ground below the Lower Side Impact Structure ( F.6.4.5, F.7.5.1 ) at its
lowest point should be 75 mm or less

input: What does rule V.1.4.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Intentional or excessive ground contact of any portion of the vehicle other than the tires will
forfeit a run or an entire dynamic event
The intent is that sliding skirts or other devices that by design, fabrication or as a consequence
of moving, contact the track surface are prohibited and any unintended contact with the
ground which causes damage, or in the opinion of the Dynamic Event Officials could result in
damage to the track, will result in forfeit of a run or an entire dynamic event

input: What does rule V.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule V.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Accommodation

input: What does rule V.2.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The vehicle must be able to accommodate drivers of sizes ranging from 5th percentile female
up to 95th percentile male.
• Accommodation includes driver position, driver controls, and driver equipment.
• Anthropometric data may be found on the FSAE Online Website.

input: What does rule V.2.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The driver’s head and hands must not contact the ground in any rollover attitude.

input: What does rule V.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Visibility
a. The driver must have sufficient visibility to the front and sides of the vehicle
b. When seated in a normal driving position, the driver must have a minimum field of vision
of 100° to both sides
c. If mirrors are required for this rule, they must remain in place and adjusted to enable the
required visibility throughout all dynamic events.

input: What does rule V.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule V.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Suspension

input: What does rule V.3.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The vehicle must have a fully operational suspension system with shock absorbers, front and
rear, with usable minimum wheel travel of 50 mm, with a driver seated.

input: What does rule V.3.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Officials may disqualify vehicles which do not represent a serious attempt at an operational
suspension system, or which demonstrate handling inappropriate for an autocross circuit.

input: What does rule V.3.1.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All suspension mounting points must be visible at Technical Inspection by direct view or by
removing any covers.

input: What does rule V.3.1.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Fasteners in the Suspension system are Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2

input: What does rule V.3.1.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
All spherical rod ends and spherical bearings on the suspension and steering must be one of:
• Mounted in double shear
• Captured by having a screw/bolt head or washer with an outside diameter that is larger
than spherical bearing housing inside diameter.

input: What does rule V.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Steering

input: What does rule V.3.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Steering Wheel must be mechanically connected to the front wheels

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 21 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule V.3.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Electrically actuated steering of the front wheels is prohibited

input: What does rule V.3.2.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Steering systems must use a rigid mechanical linkage capable of tension and compression
loads for operation

input: What does rule V.3.2.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The steering system must have positive steering stops that prevent the steering linkages from
locking up (the inversion of a four bar linkage at one of the pivots). The stops:
a. Must prevent the wheels and tires from contacting suspension, bodywork, or Chassis
during the track events
b. May be placed on the uprights or on the rack

input: What does rule V.3.2.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Allowable steering system free play is limited to seven degrees (7°) total measured at the
steering wheel.

input: What does rule V.3.2.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The steering rack must be mechanically attached to the Chassis F.5.14

input: What does rule V.3.2.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Joints between all components attaching the Steering Wheel to the steering rack must be
mechanical and be visible at Technical Inspection. Bonded joints without a mechanical backup
are not permitted.

input: What does rule V.3.2.8 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Fasteners in the steering system are Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2

input: What does rule V.3.2.9 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Spherical rod ends and spherical bearings in the steering must meet V.3.1.5 above

input: What does rule V.3.2.10 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Rear wheel steering may be used.
a. Rear wheel steering must incorporate mechanical stops to limit the range of angular
movement of the rear wheels to a maximum of six degrees (6°).
b. The team must provide the ability for the steering angle range to be verified at Technical
Inspection with a driver in the vehicle.
c. Rear wheel steering may be electrically actuated.

input: What does rule V.3.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Steering Wheel

input: What does rule V.3.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: In any angular position, the Steering Wheel must meet T.1.4.4

input: What does rule V.3.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The Steering Wheel must be attached to the column with a quick disconnect.

input: What does rule V.3.3.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The driver must be able to operate the quick disconnect while in the normal driving position
with gloves on.

input: What does rule V.3.3.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Steering Wheel must have a continuous perimeter that is near circular or near oval.
The outer perimeter profile may have some straight sections, but no concave sections. “H”,
“Figure 8”, or cutout wheels are not allowed.

input: What does rule V.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule V.4.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Wheel Size
Wheels must be 203.2 mm (8.0 inches) or more in diameter.

input: What does rule V.4.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Wheel Attachment

input: What does rule V.4.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Any wheel mounting system that uses a single retaining nut must incorporate a device to
retain the nut and the wheel if the nut loosens.
A second nut (jam nut) does not meet this requirement

input: What does rule V.4.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Teams using modified lug bolts or custom designs must provide proof that Good Engineering
Practices have been followed in their design.

input: What does rule V.4.2.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
If used, aluminum wheel nuts must be hard anodized and in pristine condition.

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 22 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule V.4.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Tires
Vehicles may have two types of tires, Dry and Wet

input: What does rule V.4.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Dry Tires
a. The tires on the vehicle when it is presented for Technical Inspection.
b. May be any size or type, slicks or treaded.

input: What does rule V.4.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Wet Tires
Any size or type of treaded or grooved tire where:
• The tread pattern or grooves were molded in by the tire manufacturer, or were cut by
the tire manufacturer or appointed agent.
Any grooves that have been cut must have documented proof that this rule was met
• There is a minimum tread depth of 2.4 mm

input: What does rule V.4.3.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Tire Set
a. All four Dry Tires and Wheels or all four Wet Tires and Wheels do not have to be
identical.
b. Once each tire set has been presented for Technical Inspection, any tire compound or
size, or wheel type or size must not be changed.

input: What does rule V.4.3.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Tire Pressure
a. Tire Pressure must be in the range allowed by the manufacturer at all times.
b. Tire Pressure may be inspected at any time

input: What does rule V.4.3.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Requirements for All Tires
a. Teams must not perform any hand cutting, grooving or modification of the tires.
b. Tire warmers are not allowed.
c. No traction enhancers may be applied to the tires at any time onsite at the competition.

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 23 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
F - CHASSIS AND STRUCTURAL

input: What does rule VE.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule VE.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Vehicle Number

input: What does rule VE.1.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The assigned vehicle number must appear on the vehicle as follows:
a. Locations: in three places, on the front of the chassis and both sides
b. Height: 150 mm minimum
c. Font: Block numbers (sans serif characters without italic, outline, shadow, or cursive
numbers)
d. Stroke Width and Spacing between numbers: 18 mm minimum
e. Color: White numbers on a black background OR black numbers on a white background
f. Background: round, oval, square or rectangular
g. Spacing: 25 mm minimum between the edge of the numbers and the edge of the
background
h. The numbers must not be obscured by parts of the vehicle

input: What does rule VE.1.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Additional letters or numerals must not show before or after the vehicle number

input: What does rule VE.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
School Name
Each vehicle must clearly display the school name.
a. Abbreviations are allowed if unique and generally recognized
b. The name must be in Roman characters minimum 50 mm high on both sides of the
vehicle.
c. The characters must be placed on a high contrast background in an easily visible location.
d. The school name may also appear in non Roman characters, but the Roman character
version must be uppermost on the sides.

input: What does rule VE.1.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
SAE Logo
The SAE International Logo must be displayed on the front and/or both sides of the vehicle in
a prominent location.

input: What does rule VE.1.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Inspection Sticker
The vehicle must have space for the Inspection Sticker(s) IN.13.2 that is:
• A clear and unobstructed area, minimum 25 cm wide x 20 cm high
• Located on the upper front surface of the nose along the vehicle centerline

input: What does rule VE.1.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Transponder / RFID Tag

input: What does rule VE.1.5.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Each vehicle must have a functional, properly mounted transponder and/or RFID tag of the
specified type(s)

input: What does rule VE.1.5.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Refer to the Rules FAQ on the FSAE Online website for transponder and RFID tag information
and mounting details

input: What does rule VE.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule VE.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Jacking Point

input: What does rule VE.2.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
A Jacking Point must be provided at the rear of the vehicle

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 75 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule VE.2.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The Jacking Point must be:
a. Capable of supporting the vehicle weight and of engaging the organizer Quick Jacks
b. Visible to a person standing 1 m behind the vehicle
c. Color: Orange
d. Oriented laterally and perpendicular to the centerline of the vehicle
e. Made from round, 25 - 30 mm OD aluminum or steel tube
f. Exposed around the lower 180° of its circumference over a minimum length of 280 mm
g. Access from the rear of the tube must be unobstructed for 300 mm or more of its length
h. The height of the tube must allow 75 mm minimum clearance from the bottom of the
tube to the ground
i. When the vehicle is raised to where the bottom of the tube is 200 mm above ground, the
wheels do not touch the ground when they are in full rebound

input: What does rule VE.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Push Bar
Each vehicle must have a removable device which attaches to the rear of the vehicle that:
a. Allows two people, standing erect behind the vehicle, to push the vehicle around the
competition site
b. Is capable of slowing and stopping the forward motion of the vehicle and pulling it
rearwards

input: What does rule VE.2.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Fire Extinguisher

input: What does rule VE.2.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Each team must have two or more fire extinguishers.
a. One extinguisher must readily be available in the team’s paddock area
b. One extinguisher must accompany the vehicle when moved using the push bar
A commercially available on board fire system may be used instead of the fire
extinguisher that accompanies the vehicle

input: What does rule VE.2.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Hand held fire extinguishers must NOT be mounted on or in the vehicle

input: What does rule VE.2.3.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Each fire extinguisher must meet the following:
a. Capacity: 0.9 kg (2 lbs)
b. Working Medium: Dry chemical/dry powder. Aqueous Film Forming Foam (AFFF) and
Halon extinguishers and systems are prohibited.
c. Equipped with a manufacturer installed pressure/charge gauge.
d. Minimum acceptable ratings:
• USA, Canada & Brazil: 10BC or 1A 10BC
• Europe: 34B or 5A 34B
• Australia: 20BE or 1A 10BE
e. Extinguishers of larger capacity (higher numerical ratings) are acceptable.

input: What does rule VE.2.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Electrical Equipment (EV Only)
The following items must accompany the vehicle at all times:
• Two pairs of High Voltage insulating gloves
• A multimeter

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 76 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023

input: What does rule VE.2.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Camera Mounts

input: What does rule VE.2.5.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: The mounts for video/photographic cameras must be of a safe and secure design.

input: What does rule VE.2.5.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: All camera installations must be approved at Technical Inspection.

input: What does rule VE.2.5.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Helmet mounted cameras and helmet camera mounts are prohibited.

input: What does rule VE.2.5.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The body of a camera or recording unit that weighs more than 0.25 kg must be secured at a
minimum of two points on different sides of the camera body.

input: What does rule VE.2.5.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
If a tether is used to restrain the camera, the tether length must be limited to prevent contact
with the driver.

input: What does rule VE.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
What does rule VE.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: General

input: What does rule VE.3.1.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Any Driver Equipment:
a. Must be in good condition with no tears, rips, open seams, areas of significant wear,
abrasions or stains which might compromise performance.
b. Must fit properly
c. May be inspected at any time

input: What does rule VE.3.1.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Flame Resistant Material
For this section some, but not all, of the approved materials are: Carbon X, Indura, Nomex,
Polybenzimidazole (common name PBI) and Proban.

input: What does rule VE.3.1.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Synthetic Material – Prohibited
Shirts, socks or other undergarments (not to be confused with flame resistant underwear)
made from nylon or any other synthetic material which could melt when exposed to high heat
are prohibited.

input: What does rule VE.3.1.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Officials may impound any non approved Driver Equipment until the end of the competition.

input: What does rule VE.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output: Helmet

input: What does rule VE.3.2.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
The driver must wear a helmet which:
a. Is closed face with an integral, immovable chin guard
b. Contains an integrated visor/face shield supplied with the helmet
c. Meets an approved standard
d. Is properly labeled for that standard

input: What does rule VE.3.2.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Acceptable helmet standards are listed below. Any additional approved standards are shown
on the Technical Inspection Form or the FAQ on the FSAE Online website.
a. Snell K2010, K2015, K2020, M2010, M2015, M2020, SA2010, SAH2010, SA2015, SA2020
b. SFI Specs 31.1/2010, 31.1/2015, 41.1/2010, 41.1/2015
c. FIA Standards FIA 8860-2004, FIA 8860-2010, FIA 8860-2018, FIA 8859-2015

input: What does rule VE.3.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Driver Gear
The driver must wear the following:

input: What does rule VE.3.3.1 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Driver Suit
A one piece suit, made from a minimum of two layers of Flame Resistant Material that covers
the body from the neck to the ankles and the wrists.

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 77 of 140
Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023
Each suit must meet one or more of the following standards and be labeled as such:
• SFI 3.2A/5 (or higher ex: /10, /15, /20)
• SFI 3.4/5 (or higher ex: /10, /15, /20)
• FIA Standard 1986
• FIA Standard 8856-2000
• FIA Standard 8856-2018

input: What does rule VE.3.3.2 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Underclothing
All competitors should wear fire retardant underwear (long pants and long sleeve shirt) under
their approved Driver Suit.

input: What does rule VE.3.3.3 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Balaclava
A Balaclava (head sock) which covers the driver’s head, hair and neck, made from Flame
Resistant Material

input: What does rule VE.3.3.4 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Socks
Socks made from Flame Resistant Material that cover the bare skin between the driver’s suit
and the Shoes.

input: What does rule VE.3.3.5 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Shoes
Shoes or boots made from Flame Resistant Material that meet an approved standard and
labeled as such:
• SFI Spec 3.3
• FIA Standard 8856-2000
• FIA Standard 8856-2018

input: What does rule VE.3.3.6 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Gloves
Gloves made from Flame Resistant Material.
Gloves of all leather construction or fire retardant gloves constructed using leather palms with
no insulating Flame Resistant Material underneath are not acceptable.

input: What does rule VE.3.3.7 is about? return only the text of the rule
output:
Arm Restraints
a. Arm restraints must be worn in a way that the driver can release them and exit the
vehicle unassisted regardless of the vehicle’s position.
b. Arm restraints must be commercially manufactured. Arm restraints certified to SFI Spec
3.3 and labeled as such meet this requirement.

Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 78 of 140

IC - INTERNAL COMBUSTION ENGINE VEHICLES
